members.summary: Rprop optimiser — Resilient Backpropagation (Riedmiller & Braun 1993).
members.extended:
A magnitude-free quasi-Newton method: each parameter carries its own
step size which grows when the gradient sign is consistent and
shrinks when it flips.  Because only $\mathrm{sign}(g)$ enters the
update, Rprop is immune to gradient-magnitude pathologies (vanishing
or exploding) but is **incompatible with mini-batch noise** — it is
a batch-mode method.

**Math**

Let $\Delta_t$ be the per-parameter step size and $g_t$ the gradient.
$$
  \Delta_{t+1} = \begin{cases}
    \min(\eta_+\,\Delta_t,\;\Delta_{\max})   & g_t\,g_{t-1} > 0 \\
    \max(\eta_-\,\Delta_t,\;\Delta_{\min})   & g_t\,g_{t-1} < 0 \\
    \Delta_t                                & g_t\,g_{t-1} = 0
  \end{cases}
$$
When the sign flips, $g_t$ is additionally **zeroed** to suppress a
secondary overshoot on the following step.  The parameter update is
$$
  \theta_{t+1} = \theta_t - \mathrm{sign}(g_t)\,\Delta_{t+1}
$$

**References**

Riedmiller & Braun, "A Direct Adaptive Method for Faster
Backpropagation Learning: The RPROP Algorithm" (ICNN 1993).

**See Also**

:class:`RMSprop` — Hinton's mini-batch-friendly replacement that
still uses an EMA of $g^2$ but keeps the gradient magnitude in the
update.

parameters.name: lr
parameters.description: Initial value placed into every element of ``step_size_``. Despite the name this is not used as a global scale.

parameters.name: eta_minus
parameters.description: Step-shrink factor $\eta_-$ applied on sign flips.

parameters.name: eta_plus
parameters.description: Step-grow factor $\eta_+$ applied on consistent signs.

notes:
Rprop assumes deterministic gradients (full-batch).  Applying it to
mini-batches confuses sign-flip detection because batch noise causes
spurious sign changes — accepted practice is to either use very
large batches or switch to RMSprop.

attributes.name: prev_grad_
attributes.description: Last-step gradient, used to detect sign flips.

attributes.name: step_size_
attributes.description: Per-element adaptive step $\Delta$.  Initialised to ``lr_`` on every element rather than to 1.0, so the very first update has a sensible scale.

methods.name: dispatch

methods.summary: Forward — calls ``IBackend::rsqrt`` to compute $y = 1/\sqrt{x}$.

methods.name: grad_formula
methods.signature: int grad_formula(const int & g)
methods.summary: Backward — $\partial y/\partial x = -\tfrac{1}{2}\,y^3$, scaled by ``grad_out``.

members.summary: Autograd node for the element-wise reciprocal square root $y = 1/\sqrt{x}$.
members.extended:
Saves the *output* $y$ so the backward pass can form $y^3$ without
re-evaluating ``rsqrt``.  Defined for $x > 0$.

**Math**

$$y = \frac{1}{\sqrt{x}}, \qquad
\frac{\partial y}{\partial x} = -\tfrac{1}{2}\,x^{-3/2} =
-\tfrac{1}{2}\,y^3, \qquad
\frac{\partial \mathcal{L}}{\partial x} =
-\tfrac{1}{2}\,y^3\,\frac{\partial \mathcal{L}}{\partial y},
\quad x > 0.$$
notes: Dispatch: Accelerate ``vvrsqrtf``/``vvrsqrt`` (CPU) / MLX ``rsqrt`` (GPU).

attributes.name: schema_v1
attributes.description: Registered as ``"rsqrt"`` with ``AmpPolicy::Promote``.

attributes.name: kSavesInput
attributes.description: ``false``.

attributes.name: kSavesOutput
attributes.description: ``true`` — saved tensor is $y = 1/\sqrt{x}$.

Dispatches to either the CPU scalar loop or the MLX GPU path based
on the parameter's device.  Implements the plain-SGD, momentum,
Nesterov, and weight-decay branches according to the constructor
flags.  See the class-level math block for the precise update
rule.

methods.signature: void init_state_slot(int slot_idx, const int & param)
methods.summary: Allocate the velocity buffer for one slot when ``momentum != 0``.

parameters.name: param
parameters.description: Parameter whose shape, dtype and device dictate the velocity buffer layout.

notes:
When ``momentum_ == 0`` the corresponding ``moment_`` entry is
left empty so that plain SGD allocates no extra memory.

members.summary: Stochastic gradient descent with optional momentum, Nesterov acceleration, and L2 weight decay.
members.extended:
Plain SGD takes the parameter $\theta$ down the negative gradient
direction at fixed learning rate $\eta$:
$$
  \theta_{t+1} = \theta_t - \eta\, g_t.
$$

With momentum coefficient $\mu > 0$, a per-parameter velocity buffer
$v$ accumulates a low-pass-filtered gradient (Polyak's heavy ball):
$$
  v_{t+1} = \mu\, v_t + (1 - \tau)\, g_t, \qquad
  \theta_{t+1} = \theta_t - \eta\, v_{t+1},
$$
where the dampening coefficient $\tau$ scales the *current* gradient
when accumulating into $v$.  ``dampening = 0`` recovers classical
Polyak momentum.

With Nesterov acceleration (Sutskever et al. 2013 reformulation),
the lookahead form is used:
$$
  v_{t+1} = \mu\, v_t + g_t, \qquad
  \theta_{t+1} = \theta_t - \eta\, (\mu\, v_{t+1} + g_t),
$$
which requires ``dampening = 0`` and ``momentum > 0``.

L2 weight decay with coefficient $\lambda$ is applied to the gradient
*before* the momentum update (PyTorch-style coupled weight decay):
$$
  g_t \leftarrow g_t + \lambda\, \theta_t.
$$
This is the standard L2 regularisation form; AdamW-style decoupled
weight decay is *not* used here — use ``AdamW`` for that variant.

**Math**

Full update (momentum branch, no Nesterov):
$$
  g_t \leftarrow g_t + \lambda\, \theta_t, \qquad
  v_{t+1} = \mu\, v_t + (1 - \tau)\, g_t, \qquad
  \theta_{t+1} = \theta_t - \eta\, v_{t+1}.
$$
Plain SGD is the $\mu = 0$ special case where the velocity buffer is
never allocated.

**References**

Polyak, "Some methods of speeding up the convergence of iteration
methods" (1964).
Sutskever, Martens, Dahl, Hinton, "On the importance of initialization
and momentum in deep learning" (ICML 2013).

**See Also**

ASGD : averaged SGD with running parameter mean.
Adam, AdamW : adaptive moment optimisers.
examples: Typical training-loop usage from Python:

>>> opt = lucid.optim.SGD(model.parameters(), lr=0.01,
...                       momentum=0.9, weight_decay=1e-4)
>>> opt.zero_grad()
>>> loss.backward()
>>> opt.step()
notes:
CPU and GPU update paths are dispatched inside ``update_one`` based
on the parameter's device.  The CPU path is a flat scalar loop over
the raw byte buffer; the GPU path expresses the update as a few MLX
array ops, which compose with the surrounding lazy graph.

attributes.name: lr_
attributes.description: Learning rate $\eta$.  Must be non-negative.  Updated by schedulers via ``set_lr``.

attributes.name: momentum_
attributes.description: Momentum coefficient $\mu$.  Zero disables momentum and skips velocity-buffer allocation.

attributes.name: dampening_
attributes.description: Dampening coefficient $\tau$.  Must be zero when ``nesterov_`` is true.

attributes.name: weight_decay_
attributes.description: L2 penalty coefficient $\lambda$.

attributes.name: nesterov_
attributes.description: Enables the Nesterov lookahead form.  Requires ``momentum_ > 0`` and ``dampening_ == 0``.

attributes.name: moment_
attributes.description: Per-parameter velocity buffers.  Entry $i$ is allocated by ``init_state_slot`` on the first step seen by slot $i$ only when ``momentum_ != 0``; otherwise it remains empty.

members.name: ScaledDotProductAttentionBackward
members.signature: ScaledDotProductAttentionBackward

methods.name: forward
methods.signature: int forward(const int & q, const int & k, const int & v, const int & attn_mask_or_null, double scale, bool is_causal)
methods.summary: Run the SDPA forward and return the output tensor.
methods.extended:
Dispatches to ``IBackend::sdpa_forward`` after validating that the
leading batch dims of ``q``, ``k`` and ``v`` agree, that
``q.shape[-1] == k.shape[-1]`` (the dot-product key dim ``d_k``),
and that ``k.shape[-2] == v.shape[-2]`` (the source-sequence
length ``L_k``).  On the autograd-recording path this also
attaches a new ``ScaledDotProductAttentionBackward`` node with
``saved_weights_`` populated.

parameters.name: q
parameters.description: Query tensor of shape ``(..., L_q, d_k)`` with at least 2 dimensions.  All leading "batch" dims must broadcast with the corresponding dims of ``k`` and ``v``.

parameters.name: k
parameters.description: Key tensor of shape ``(..., L_k, d_k)``; the last dim must equal ``q.shape[-1]``.

parameters.name: v
parameters.description: Value tensor of shape ``(..., L_k, d_v)``; the penultimate dim must equal ``k.shape[-2]``.

parameters.name: attn_mask_or_null
parameters.description: Optional additive mask broadcastable to ``(..., L_q, L_k)``. Pass ``nullptr`` to omit (no additive term is materialised).

parameters.name: scale
parameters.description: Multiplicative factor applied to ``Q @ K^T`` before the softmax.  Typically $1/\sqrt{d_k}$.

parameters.name: is_causal
parameters.description: If ``true``, an upper-triangular ``-inf`` mask is added engine-side, restricting position ``i`` to attend only to positions ``j <= i``.

returns.description: Attention output of shape ``(..., L_q, d_v)``.

raises.description: If any input is null, devices or dtypes disagree, rank is less than 2, or the shape contracts above are violated.

methods.name: apply
methods.signature: int apply(int grad_out)
methods.summary: Compute gradients with respect to ``Q``, ``K`` and ``V`` from the upstream gradient of the output.
methods.extended:
Reconstructs the saved weight matrix on the GPU path (where
``saved_weights_`` is a ``{1}``-shape placeholder) by recomputing
$W = \text{softmax}(QK^\top \cdot s)$, then dispatches to
``IBackend::sdpa_backward`` for the three input gradients.

parameters.name: grad_out
parameters.description: Upstream gradient $\partial \mathcal{L}/\partial y$ shaped like the SDPA output ``(..., L_q, d_v)``.

returns.description: Three Storage objects in edge order ``{dQ, dK, dV}``, each reshaped back to ``orig_q_shape_`` / ``orig_k_shape_`` / ``orig_v_shape_``.

members.summary: Autograd node for scaled dot-product attention with optional additive mask and post-softmax causal constraint.
members.extended:
Implements the canonical SDPA operation from [Vaswani et al. (2017)](https://arxiv.org/abs/1706.03762),
$\text{Attn}(Q, K, V) = \text{softmax}\!\left(\frac{QK^\top}{\sqrt{d_k}} + M\right)V$,
where $Q, K, V$ are query / key / value projections shaped
``(..., L, D)`` and $M$ is an additive mask (``-inf`` at disallowed
positions, ``0`` elsewhere).  The node saves three edges
``{Q, K, V}`` and a fourth Storage slot ``saved_weights_`` holding the
pre-multiplication attention weight matrix
$W = \text{softmax}(QK^\top \cdot s)$, where ``s = scale_``.

On the MLX (GPU) path the fused ``mlx::core::fast::scaled_dot_product_attention``
kernel does not return $W$ — instead it returns a ``{1}``-shape
placeholder.  ``apply()`` detects ``saved_weights_.shape().size() == 1``
and re-computes $W = \text{softmax}(QK^\top \cdot s)$ from the saved
$Q, K$ before back-propagating.  On the Accelerate (CPU) path the
kernel returns the genuine weight matrix and the recompute branch is
skipped.

**Math**

$$
  A_{ij} = s \cdot (Q_i \cdot K_j), \quad
  P_{ij} = \frac{\exp(A_{ij} + M_{ij})}{\sum_k \exp(A_{ik} + M_{ik})}, \quad
  y_i = \sum_j P_{ij}\, V_j
$$
Gradients flow back through the softmax Jacobian
$\partial P / \partial A = \mathrm{diag}(P) - P P^\top$ (per row),
giving
$$
  \frac{\partial \mathcal{L}}{\partial Q} = s \cdot (\Delta A)\, K,\quad
  \frac{\partial \mathcal{L}}{\partial K} = s \cdot (\Delta A)^\top Q,\quad
  \frac{\partial \mathcal{L}}{\partial V} = P^\top\, \frac{\partial \mathcal{L}}{\partial y}
$$
where $\Delta A_{ij} = P_{ij}\bigl(\partial \mathcal{L}/\partial y_i \cdot V_j^\top - \sum_k P_{ik} (\partial \mathcal{L}/\partial y_i \cdot V_k^\top)\bigr)$.

**References**

Vaswani et al., "Attention Is All You Need" (NeurIPS 2017).
notes:
The mask is *additive*: callers must already have converted boolean
"where to mask" patterns into floats containing ``-inf`` where the
position should be excluded and ``0`` elsewhere (see
:func:`lucid.nn.modules.attention._to_additive_mask`).  Causal masking
is implemented engine-side when ``is_causal=true`` by adding an
upper-triangular ``-inf`` matrix before the softmax, so the caller
does not need to materialise the mask in Python.

attributes.name: schema_v1
attributes.description: Op schema (name ``"scaled_dot_product_attention"``, version 1, ``AmpPolicy::ForceFP32``, autograd-aware).  AMP forces the kernel to FP32 because the softmax-exp accumulation is numerically unstable at half precision.

attributes.name: scale_
attributes.description: Dot-product scale factor ``s`` (typically $1/\sqrt{d_k}$) multiplied into $QK^\top$ before the softmax.  Default: ``1.0``.

attributes.name: orig_q_shape_
attributes.description: Original (unflattened) shape of ``Q`` recorded at forward; used by ``apply()`` to restore leading batch dims on the gradient tensor.

attributes.name: orig_k_shape_
attributes.description: Original shape of ``K``.

attributes.name: orig_v_shape_
attributes.description: Original shape of ``V``.

attributes.name: saved_weights_
attributes.description: Attention weight matrix ``W``.  Genuine on the CPU path; on the GPU path this is a ``{1}``-shape placeholder and the backward recomputes ``W`` from the saved ``Q``, ``K`` and ``scale_``.

parameters.name: schema

methods.name: effective_dtype
methods.signature: Dtype effective_dtype()
methods.summary: Returns the dtype that the op should use for its computation.
methods.extended:
May differ from the ``input_dtype`` passed at construction when
AMP is active and the schema's :enum:`AmpPolicy` is ``Promote``
(returns the autocast dtype, possibly demoted on CPU) or
``ForceFP32`` (returns :class:`Dtype::F32`).  Equals
``input_dtype`` for ``KeepInput`` and when AMP is not active.

returns.description: The effective compute dtype for the op.

notes:
Marked ``noexcept`` — purely a field read, no allocations or
exceptions.

members.summary: Combined determinism + AMP dtype-resolution guard.
members.extended:
Constructed once at the top of an op's dispatch routine.  The
constructor performs both checks; subsequent code reads the resolved
dtype via :func:`effective_dtype`.

Despite the "Guard" suffix this is not an RAII resource holder — it
owns no locks, allocations, or thread-local state.  The name reflects
its role as a gate at the entry of the op, not a scope object.

**See Also**

:func:`check_schema_determinism` — determinism-only variant.
examples: .. code-block:: cpp

methods.name: dispatch
methods.signature: int dispatch(backend::IBackend & be, const int & a, const int & s, Dtype dt)
methods.summary: Forward — calls ``IBackend::selu`` to compute the Scaled ELU $y = \lambda\,x$ for $x \ge 0$ else $\lambda\,\alpha\,(e^x - 1)$.

methods.name: grad_formula
methods.signature: int grad_formula(const int & g)
methods.summary: Eager-mode backward: dispatches to ``IBackend::selu_backward``.

members.summary: Autograd node for SELU (Scaled ELU, Klambauer et al. 2017).
members.extended:
Uses the self-normalising constants $\alpha \approx 1.6732632$ and
$\lambda \approx 1.0507010$ baked into the backend; the Python and C++
layers do not expose them to the caller.  The backward delegates to the
backend because the piecewise scaled-ELU formula depends on those fixed
constants.

**Math**

$$y = \lambda\,\begin{cases} x, & x \ge 0 \\
                             \alpha\,(e^x - 1), & x < 0 \end{cases}$$

**References**

Klambauer et al., "Self-Normalizing Neural Networks", [arXiv:1706.02515](https://arxiv.org/abs/1706.02515).

attributes.name: schema_v1
attributes.description: ``"selu"``, ``AmpPolicy::ForceFP32``.

members.summary: Thrown when two tensors have shapes that are incompatible with an operation's broadcasting or contraction rules.
members.extended:
The expected and actual shapes are retained as integer vectors so callers
can inspect them programmatically rather than parsing ``what()``.  The
message string formatted by the constructor includes both shapes plus the
``context`` annotation supplied by :class:`ErrorBuilder`.  Mapped to
``lucid._C.engine.ShapeMismatch`` on the Python side.

**See Also**

:class:`Validator` : Wraps the common precondition checks that raise this
    exception via :class:`ErrorBuilder`.

parameters.name: context
parameters.description: Free-form annotation produced by :class:`ErrorBuilder` (typically the op name plus the optional ``detail`` argument and any thread-local trace).

attributes.description: Stored copy of the required shape.

attributes.name: got_
attributes.description: Stored copy of the offending shape.

methods.name: get_version
methods.signature: int get_version()
methods.summary: Reads the current version counter value.

returns.description: Monotonically non-decreasing snapshot of mutation count.

methods.name: cpu_view
methods.signature: CpuStorage cpu_view()
methods.summary: Returns a :class:`CpuStorage` that aliases ``cpu_ptr`` without copying bytes.
methods.extended:
The returned storage installs a custom deleter that holds a copy of
:attr:`owner`, guaranteeing the Metal buffer outlives every
:class:`CpuStorage` view spawned this way.  The :type:`VersionCounter`
is also shared with this :class:`SharedStorage`, so mutations through
the CPU view are visible to the GPU side.

returns.description: CPU view aliasing the shared buffer.  Lifetime-tied to ``owner``.

members.summary: Metal shared-memory storage accessible from both CPU and GPU.
members.extended:
The underlying allocation is a Metal buffer created with
``MTLResourceStorageModeShared``, which the Metal driver maps into the
process address space.  This gives Lucid a zero-copy host↔device bridge on
Apple Silicon unified-memory hardware: the same bytes are addressable from
CPU code through ``cpu_ptr`` and submitted to the GPU through
``mtl_handle``.

**See Also**

:func:`cpu_view` — produces a :class:`CpuStorage` aliasing this buffer.

attributes.name: cpu_ptr
attributes.description: Host-side mapped pointer.  Safe to dereference from any CPU thread.

attributes.name: mtl_handle
attributes.description: Opaque ``id<MTLBuffer>`` handle, stored as ``void*`` to avoid pulling the Metal headers into this translation unit.

attributes.name: nbytes
attributes.description: Allocation capacity in bytes.

attributes.name: dtype
attributes.description: Element type.  Default :attr:`Dtype::F32`.

attributes.name: version
attributes.description: Shared mutation counter — same semantics as the other variants.

attributes.name: owner
attributes.description: Lifetime token for the underlying Metal allocation.  The :func:`cpu_view` deleter captures a copy so the Metal buffer cannot be freed while a CPU view is alive.

members.name: SigmoidBackward
members.signature: SigmoidBackward

methods.name: dispatch
methods.signature: int dispatch(backend::IBackend & be, const int & a, const int & s, Dtype dt)
methods.summary: Forward — calls ``IBackend::sigmoid`` to compute $y = 1 / (1 + e^{-x})$.

methods.name: grad_formula
methods.signature: int grad_formula(const int & g)
methods.summary: Eager-mode backward: $\partial L/\partial x = y(1 - y) \cdot \partial L/\partial y$ using the saved output $y$.

methods.name: grad_formula_impl
methods.signature: int grad_formula_impl(const int & g, const int &, const int & out)
methods.summary: Graph-mode backward: ``out * (1 - out) * g`` expressed as composable ops so the chain remains differentiable.

members.summary: Autograd node for the logistic sigmoid $y = 1 / (1 + e^{-x})$.
members.extended:
Saves the *output* $y$ rather than the input because the gradient formula
$y(1 - y)$ depends only on $y$.  This avoids a redundant forward pass and
keeps the saved tensor at the same dtype as the activation.

**Math**

$$y = \frac{1}{1 + e^{-x}}, \qquad \frac{\partial y}{\partial x} = y\,(1 - y)$$
notes:
The eager backward builds $(1 - y)$ via ``mul_scalar(-1) + add_scalar(1)``
to reuse the existing storage primitives rather than introducing a dedicated
subtract kernel.

attributes.name: schema_v1
attributes.description: ``"sigmoid"``, ``AmpPolicy::Promote`` — half-precision inputs are promoted to float32 to keep $e^{-x}$ from underflowing for large $|x|$.

members.name: SignBackward
members.signature: SignBackward

methods.name: dispatch
methods.signature: int dispatch(backend::IBackend & be, const int & a, const int & s, Dtype dt)
methods.summary: Forward — calls ``IBackend::sign`` to compute $y = \mathrm{sign}(x) \in \{-1, 0, +1\}$.

methods.name: grad_formula
methods.signature: int grad_formula(const int & g)
methods.summary: Backward — $\partial y/\partial x = 0$ almost everywhere; returns an empty zero-sentinel since no autograd edge is wired.

members.summary: Autograd node for the element-wise sign function $y = \mathrm{sign}(x) \in \{-1, 0, +1\}$.
members.extended:
Because ``sign`` is piecewise constant its derivative vanishes almost
everywhere; the node therefore carries no gradient information.
``kHasGradient = false`` tells :class:`UnaryKernel::forward` to skip the
autograd wiring entirely, and ``grad_formula`` returns an empty
``CpuStorage`` only as a defensive zero-sentinel if it is ever invoked
manually.

**Math**

$$y = \mathrm{sign}(x), \qquad
\frac{\partial y}{\partial x} = 0 \text{ almost everywhere}.$$
notes: Dispatch: Accelerate sign kernel (CPU) / MLX ``sign`` (GPU).

attributes.name: schema_v1
attributes.description: Registered as ``"sign"`` with ``AmpPolicy::KeepInput`` — integer dtypes pass through unmodified.

attributes.name: kSavesInput
attributes.description: ``false``.

attributes.name: kHasGradient
attributes.description: ``false`` — no autograd edge is created.

members.name: SiluBackward
members.signature: SiluBackward

methods.name: dispatch
methods.signature: int dispatch(backend::IBackend & be, const int & a, const int & s, Dtype dt)
methods.summary: Forward — calls ``IBackend::silu`` to compute $y = x \cdot \sigma(x)$.

methods.name: grad_formula
methods.signature: int grad_formula(const int & g)
methods.summary: Eager-mode backward: dispatches to ``IBackend::silu_backward`` with the saved input and the upstream gradient.

members.summary: Autograd node for the SiLU / Swish activation $y = x \cdot \sigma(x)$ (Hendrycks & Gimpel 2016 / Ramachandran et al. 2017).
members.extended:
Saves the input $x$; the backward delegates to the backend so the fused
gradient kernel ($\sigma(x)\,(1 + x(1 - \sigma(x)))$) runs as a single pass
on MLX or a single Accelerate composition on CPU rather than the prior
seven-op storage-primitive expansion.

**Math**

$$y = x\,\sigma(x), \qquad \frac{\partial y}{\partial x}
  = \sigma(x)\,\bigl(1 + x\,(1 - \sigma(x))\bigr)$$

**References**

Hendrycks & Gimpel, "Gaussian Error Linear Units (GELUs)", 2016.
Ramachandran et al., "Searching for Activation Functions", 2017.

attributes.name: schema_v1
attributes.description: ``"silu"``, ``AmpPolicy::Promote``.

members.name: SinBackward
members.signature: SinBackward

methods.name: dispatch
methods.signature: int dispatch(backend::IBackend & be, const int & a, const int & s, Dtype dt)
methods.summary: Forward — calls ``IBackend::sin`` to compute $y = \sin x$.

methods.name: grad_formula
methods.signature: int grad_formula(const int & g)
methods.summary: Backward — $\partial y/\partial x = \cos x$, scaled by ``grad_out``.

members.summary: Autograd node for element-wise sine $y = \sin(x)$.
members.extended:
Saves the input ``x`` so the backward pass can evaluate $\cos(x)$ and
scale the upstream gradient by it.  The input is interpreted in radians.

**Math**

$$y = \sin(x), \qquad
\frac{\partial y}{\partial x} = \cos(x), \qquad
\frac{\partial \mathcal{L}}{\partial x} =
\cos(x)\,\frac{\partial \mathcal{L}}{\partial y}.$$
notes: Dispatch: Accelerate ``vvsinf``/``vvsin`` (CPU) / MLX ``sin`` (GPU).

attributes.name: schema_v1
attributes.description: Registered as ``"sin"`` with ``AmpPolicy::Promote``.

members.name: SinhBackward
members.signature: SinhBackward

methods.name: dispatch
methods.signature: int dispatch(backend::IBackend & be, const int & a, const int & s, Dtype dt)
methods.summary: Forward — calls ``IBackend::sinh`` to compute $y = \sinh x$.

methods.name: grad_formula
methods.signature: int grad_formula(const int & g)
methods.summary: Backward — $\partial y/\partial x = \cosh x$, scaled by ``grad_out``.

members.summary: Autograd node for element-wise hyperbolic sine $y = \sinh(x)$.
members.extended:
Saves the input ``x`` so the backward pass can evaluate $\cosh(x)$ and
scale the upstream gradient by it.  Defined on all of $\mathbb{R}$ with
the identity $\sinh(x) = (e^x - e^{-x})/2$.

**Math**

$$y = \sinh(x), \qquad
\frac{\partial y}{\partial x} = \cosh(x), \qquad
\frac{\partial \mathcal{L}}{\partial x} =
\cosh(x)\,\frac{\partial \mathcal{L}}{\partial y}.$$
notes:
Dispatch: Accelerate ``vvsinhf``/``vvsinh`` (CPU) / MLX ``sinh`` (GPU).
Output magnitude grows exponentially; large-magnitude inputs can overflow
to ``+inf`` / ``-inf``.

attributes.name: schema_v1
attributes.description: Registered as ``"sinh"`` with ``AmpPolicy::Promote``.

members.name: SoftmaxBackward
members.signature: SoftmaxBackward

methods.name: forward
methods.signature: int forward(const int & a, int axis)
methods.summary: Validate the axis, dispatch the forward softmax through the backend, save the resulting probabilities on the node, and wire the autograd edges back to ``a``.

parameters.name: axis
parameters.description: Softmax axis; negative values are normalised to ``axis + ndim``.

returns.description: Output probabilities $p$ with the same shape and dtype as ``a``.

raises.description: If ``axis`` is out of range after normalisation.

methods.name: apply
methods.signature: int apply(int grad_out)
methods.summary: Eager-mode backward: dispatches to ``IBackend::softmax_backward`` which evaluates $p \odot (\partial L/\partial y - \langle p, \partial L/\partial y\rangle)$ in a single fused pass along ``axis_``.

members.summary: Autograd node for the numerically stable softmax along a single axis $p_i = e^{x_i - m} / \sum_j e^{x_j - m}$ with $m = \max_j x_j$.
members.extended:
Subtracting the per-row maximum $m$ before exponentiating is the standard
log-sum-exp trick — it preserves the analytic result while keeping every
exponentiated term in $(0, 1]$, which is essential under ``ForceFP32``
AMP semantics for fp16/bf16 logits.  The softmax probabilities $p$ are
saved on the node so the backward needs no re-evaluation.

**Math**

$$p_i = \frac{e^{x_i}}{\sum_j e^{x_j}},
  \qquad \frac{\partial L}{\partial x_i}
  = p_i\,\Bigl(\frac{\partial L}{\partial y_i}
               - \sum_j p_j\,\frac{\partial L}{\partial y_j}\Bigr)$$

The Jacobian-vector form sidesteps materialising the dense
$N \times N$ softmax Jacobian and runs in $O(N)$ per row.

**Shape**

Input and output share the same shape; the normalisation contracts only
along ``axis_``.  Negative axis values are normalised to non-negative
indices by ``forward`` before being stored.

**References**

Bishop, "Pattern Recognition and Machine Learning", §4.3.4.

raises.description: If the normalised axis falls outside ``[0, ndim)``.

attributes.name: schema_v1
attributes.description: ``"softmax"``, ``AmpPolicy::ForceFP32`` (probability underflow on float16 is unsafe for large logit magnitudes).

attributes.name: axis_
attributes.description: Canonical (non-negative) axis along which the softmax is computed.

attributes.name: saved_output_
attributes.description: Softmax probabilities $p$ produced by ``forward``, used by ``apply``.

members.signature: SoftplusBackward

methods.name: dispatch
methods.signature: int dispatch(backend::IBackend & be, const int & a, const int & s, Dtype dt)
methods.summary: Forward — calls ``IBackend::softplus`` to compute $y = \log(1 + e^x)$.

methods.name: grad_formula
methods.signature: int grad_formula(const int & g)
methods.summary: Eager-mode backward: $\partial L/\partial x = \sigma(x) \cdot \partial L/\partial y$, evaluated via the storage-level sigmoid kernel applied to the saved input.

members.summary: Autograd node for Softplus $y = \log(1 + e^x)$ — the smooth surrogate of ReLU whose derivative is the logistic sigmoid.
members.extended:
Saves the input so ``grad_formula`` can recompute $\sigma(x)$ via the
storage-level sigmoid primitive (a single MLX or Accelerate call).

**Math**

$$y = \log(1 + e^x), \qquad \frac{\partial y}{\partial x} = \sigma(x)$$

attributes.name: schema_v1
attributes.description: ``"softplus"``, ``AmpPolicy::ForceFP32`` — guards against $e^x$ overflow in float16 for $|x| > 11$ or so.

members.name: SolveBackward
members.signature: SolveBackward

methods.name: apply
methods.signature: int apply(int grad_out)
methods.summary: Compute input gradients for the forward solve $AX = B$.

parameters.name: grad_out
parameters.description: Upstream gradient $G = \partial L / \partial X$, shaped like the forward output $X$.

returns.description: Two-element vector ``{dA, dB}`` matching the input ordering ``[A=0, B=1]`` that ``NaryKernel`` expects, where ``dB = solve(Aᵀ, G)`` and ``dA = -dB @ Xᵀ``.

notes:
The transpose solve ``solve(Aᵀ, G)`` performs a fresh LU
factorisation of $A^\top$ — the forward factors are not cached.

members.summary: Autograd node for the general linear solve $AX = B$.
members.extended:
The forward saves $A$ as ``saved_inputs_[0]`` (required to build
$A^\top$ for the adjoint solve) and the solution $X$ as
``saved_output_`` (required for the outer-product step that yields
$\partial L / \partial A$).  Although $B$ is technically saved by
``NaryKernel`` when ``save_inputs=true``, the backward never reads
it.

**Math**

Given upstream gradient $G = \partial L / \partial X$:
$$
  \frac{\partial L}{\partial B} = \text{solve}(A^\top, G), \qquad
  \frac{\partial L}{\partial A} = -\,\frac{\partial L}{\partial B}\, X^\top
$$
Both expressions compose existing differentiable ops
([[solve_op]] / [[matmul_op]] / [[neg_op]] / [[mT_op]]) so that
second-order gradients flow automatically.

**References**

Giles, "Collected Matrix Derivative Results for Forward and Reverse
Mode Algorithmic Differentiation" (Oxford, 2008), §2.4.

attributes.name: schema_v1
attributes.description: ``"solve"``, one saved-input slot, ``AmpPolicy::KeepInput`` — prevents a lossy autocast promotion before the LAPACK call.

members.name: SqrtBackward
members.signature: SqrtBackward

methods.name: dispatch
methods.signature: int dispatch(backend::IBackend & be, const int & a, const int & shape, Dtype dt)
methods.summary: Forward — calls ``IBackend::sqrt`` to compute $y = \sqrt{x}$.

methods.name: grad_formula
methods.signature: int grad_formula(const int & g)
methods.summary: Backward — $\partial y/\partial x = 1/(2y)$, scaled by ``grad_out``.

methods.name: grad_formula_impl
methods.signature: int grad_formula_impl(const int & g, const int &, const int & out)
methods.summary: Graph-mode backward: $\partial x = g / (2y)$ where $y = \sqrt{x}$ is the saved output; kept inside the autograd graph.

members.summary: Autograd node for the element-wise square root $y = \sqrt{x}$.
members.extended:
Saves the *output* $y$ because the backward formula
$\partial y / \partial x = 1 / (2y)$ is cheaper and numerically
identical to recomputing $\sqrt{x}$.  Defined for $x \geq 0$; negative
inputs produce NaN.

**Math**

$$y = \sqrt{x}, \qquad
\frac{\partial y}{\partial x} = \frac{1}{2\sqrt{x}} = \frac{1}{2y},
\qquad
\frac{\partial \mathcal{L}}{\partial x} =
\frac{1}{2y}\,\frac{\partial \mathcal{L}}{\partial y}, \quad x \geq 0.$$
notes:
Dispatch: Accelerate ``vvsqrtf``/``vvsqrt`` (CPU) / MLX ``sqrt`` (GPU).
Gradient is unbounded as $x \to 0^+$.

attributes.name: schema_v1
attributes.description: Registered as ``"sqrt"`` with ``AmpPolicy::Promote``.

attributes.name: kSavesInput
attributes.description: ``false``.

attributes.name: kSavesOutput
attributes.description: ``true`` — saved tensor is $y = \sqrt{x}$.

methods.summary: Graph-mode backward: $\partial x = 2x \cdot g$ kept inside the autograd graph.

members.summary: Autograd node for the element-wise square $y = x^2$.
members.extended:
Saves the input ``x`` so the backward pass can scale the upstream
gradient by $2x$.

**Math**

$$y = x^2, \qquad
\frac{\partial y}{\partial x} = 2x, \qquad
\frac{\partial \mathcal{L}}{\partial x} =
2x\,\frac{\partial \mathcal{L}}{\partial y}.$$
notes: Dispatch: Accelerate ``vDSP_vsq`` (CPU) / MLX ``square`` (GPU).

methods.summary: Build a step-decay schedule.

methods.summary: Evaluate the step-decay formula at ``epoch``.

returns.description: ``base_lr_ * gamma_ ** (epoch / step_size_)``.

members.summary: Step decay — multiply lr by $\gamma$ every ``step_size`` epochs.
members.extended:
**Math**

$$
  \eta_t = \eta_0 \cdot \gamma^{\lfloor t / s \rfloor}
$$
where $s$ is ``step_size`` and $\eta_0$ is ``base_lr_``.

**References**

Classical staircase schedule used since AlexNet (Krizhevsky 2012).

attributes.name: step_size_
attributes.description: Period in epochs between successive decays.

attributes.name: gamma_
attributes.description: Multiplicative decay factor.

methods.name: dispatch
methods.signature: int dispatch(backend::IBackend & be, const int & a, const int & b, const int & shape, Dtype dt)
methods.summary: Forward dispatch hook called by BinaryKernel::forward.

parameters.name: be
parameters.description: Active backend (CPU Accelerate or GPU MLX).

returns.description: Output storage holding $a - b$.

methods.name: grad_formula
methods.signature: int grad_formula(const int & grad_out)
methods.summary: Compute the storage-level gradients for both operands.
methods.extended:
**Math**

$$
  \mathrm{dA} = \frac{\partial L}{\partial y}, \qquad
  \mathrm{dB} = -\frac{\partial L}{\partial y}
$$

parameters.name: grad_out
parameters.description: Upstream gradient at the broadcasted output shape.

returns.description: ``(dA, dB)`` where ``dA`` is a clone of ``grad_out`` and ``dB`` is its element-wise negation.

methods.name: grad_formula_impl
methods.signature: int grad_formula_impl(const int & grad_out, const int &, const int &)
methods.summary: Graph-mode (TensorImpl) variant of the gradient.

parameters.name: a, b
parameters.description: Forward inputs (unused — values irrelevant to subtraction gradient).

returns.description: ``(grad_out, -grad_out)``.

members.summary: Autograd node for element-wise subtraction $y = a - b$ with NumPy broadcasting.
members.extended:
Inherits forward dispatch, broadcasting logic, and the apply() trampoline
from BinaryOp<SubBackward>.  No forward inputs are saved because the
gradient depends only on the upstream gradient: $a$ receives it unchanged
and $b$ receives it negated.  Broadcast-reduces each branch back to the
original input shape via :func:`sum_to_shape` inside
:func:`BinaryKernel::apply`.

**Math**

$$
  y = a - b, \qquad
  \frac{\partial L}{\partial a} = \frac{\partial L}{\partial y}, \qquad
  \frac{\partial L}{\partial b} = -\frac{\partial L}{\partial y}
$$

**Shape**

Inputs follow NumPy broadcasting; output has the broadcasted shape.  Each
gradient is then reduced to the original input's shape.

**See Also**

AddBackward, MulBackward, DivBackward
notes:
CPU dispatch uses Accelerate ``vDSP_vsub`` / ``vDSP_vsubD`` for F32 / F64.
GPU dispatch uses MLX's broadcast-aware ``subtract`` primitive.

attributes.name: kSavesInputs
attributes.description: ``false`` — gradient is value-independent.

attributes.name: schema_v1
attributes.description: ``"sub"``, schema version 1, ``AmpPolicy::Promote``, deterministic.

members.name: SumBackward
members.signature: SumBackward

methods.name: dispatch
methods.signature: int dispatch(backend::IBackend & be, const int & a, const int & in_shape, const int & axes, bool keepdims, Dtype dt)
methods.summary: Forward — reduces ``a`` along ``axes`` via ``IBackend::reduce_sum``.

methods.name: grad_formula
methods.signature: int grad_formula(const int & grad_out)
methods.summary: Backward — broadcasts ``grad_out`` back to the input shape (ones gradient).

methods.name: scale_graph_grad
methods.signature: int scale_graph_grad(const int & g)
methods.summary: Graph-mode scaling helper — ``sum`` requires no scaling, so the broadcast-expanded gradient is returned unchanged.

members.summary: Autograd node for the multi-axis reduction sum $y = \sum_{i \in \text{axes}} x_i$.
members.extended:
The gradient broadcasts unchanged from the reduced shape back to the
input shape because $\partial y / \partial x_i = 1$ for every element
participating in the sum.  ``kSavesInput = false`` and no output is
saved — the rule is independent of both the forward input and output.

**Math**

$$
  y = \sum_{i \in \text{axes}} x_i, \qquad
  \frac{\partial \mathcal{L}}{\partial x_i} =
  \mathrm{broadcast}\!\left(\frac{\partial \mathcal{L}}{\partial y}
  \right).
$$
notes:
Dispatch: Accelerate strided reductions (CPU) / MLX ``sum`` (GPU).
For empty reductions the output is $0$ (additive identity).

attributes.name: schema_v1
attributes.description: Registered as ``"sum"`` with ``AmpPolicy::Promote`` — integer and boolean inputs are promoted to ``int64`` before reduction so the sum cannot overflow narrow integer dtypes (parity fix found during the M4 Max training sanity check, where ``bool.sum()`` previously dropped to ``any()`` semantics on GPU).

attributes.name: kSavesInput
attributes.description: ``false``.  Backward depends only on shape metadata.

members.name: TanBackward
members.signature: TanBackward

methods.name: dispatch
methods.signature: int dispatch(backend::IBackend & be, const int & a, const int & s, Dtype dt)
methods.summary: Forward — calls ``IBackend::tan`` to compute $y = \tan x$.

methods.name: grad_formula
methods.signature: int grad_formula(const int & g)
methods.summary: Backward — $\partial y/\partial x = \sec^2 x$, scaled by ``grad_out``.

members.summary: Autograd node for element-wise tangent $y = \tan(x)$.
members.extended:
Saves the input ``x`` so the backward pass can build $\cos^2(x)$ in the
denominator.  Defined for all real $x$ except the singular points
$x = (k + \tfrac{1}{2})\pi$ where $\cos x = 0$; behaviour at those
points is undefined.

**Math**

$$y = \tan(x), \qquad
\frac{\partial y}{\partial x} = \sec^2(x) = \frac{1}{\cos^2(x)}, \qquad
\frac{\partial \mathcal{L}}{\partial x} =
\frac{1}{\cos^2(x)}\,\frac{\partial \mathcal{L}}{\partial y}.$$
notes: Dispatch: Accelerate ``vvtanf``/``vvtan`` (CPU) / MLX ``tan`` (GPU).

attributes.name: schema_v1
attributes.description: Registered as ``"tan"`` with ``AmpPolicy::Promote``.

members.name: TanhBackward
members.signature: TanhBackward

methods.name: dispatch
methods.signature: int dispatch(backend::IBackend & be, const int & a, const int & shape, Dtype dt)
methods.summary: Forward — calls ``IBackend::tanh`` to compute $y = \tanh x$.

methods.name: grad_formula
methods.signature: int grad_formula(const int & g)
methods.summary: Backward — $\partial y/\partial x = 1 - y^2$ using the saved output, scaled by ``grad_out``.

methods.name: grad_formula_impl
methods.signature: int grad_formula_impl(const int & g, const int &, const int & out)
methods.summary: Graph-mode backward: $\partial x = (1 - \mathrm{out}^2) \cdot g$ where $\mathrm{out} = \tanh x$; kept inside the autograd graph.

members.summary: Autograd node for element-wise hyperbolic tangent $y = \tanh(x)$.
members.extended:
Saves the *output* $y$ rather than the input because the backward
formula $1 - y^2$ is cheaper than re-evaluating $\tanh(x)$ and avoids a
second ``vvtanhf`` pass.  ``kSavesInput = false`` opts out of the
default input-save behaviour and ``kSavesOutput = true`` registers the
output instead.  Range is $(-1, 1)$ on all of $\mathbb{R}$.

**Math**

$$y = \tanh(x), \qquad
\frac{\partial y}{\partial x} = 1 - \tanh^2(x) = 1 - y^2, \qquad
\frac{\partial \mathcal{L}}{\partial x} =
(1 - y^2)\,\frac{\partial \mathcal{L}}{\partial y}.$$
notes: Dispatch: Accelerate ``vvtanhf``/``vvtanh`` (CPU) / MLX ``tanh`` (GPU).

attributes.name: schema_v1
attributes.description: Registered as ``"tanh"`` with ``AmpPolicy::Promote``.

attributes.name: kSavesInput
attributes.description: ``false``.

attributes.name: kSavesOutput
attributes.description: ``true`` — the saved tensor is $y = \tanh(x)$.

methods.name: TensorImpl
methods.signature: void TensorImpl(int storage, int shape, Dtype dtype, Device device, bool requires_grad)
methods.summary: Constructs a :class:`TensorImpl` from an already-allocated :class:`Storage`.
methods.extended:
The C-contiguous (row-major) stride is computed automatically from
``shape`` and ``dtype``.  When ``requires_grad`` is ``true`` an
:class:`AutogradMeta` is constructed immediately; otherwise it is
left unallocated until needed.

parameters.name: storage
parameters.description: Backing memory (any variant).  Ownership is moved in.

parameters.name: shape
parameters.description: Dimension vector.  May be empty for a scalar.

parameters.name: device
parameters.description: CPU or GPU.  Must match the variant of ``storage``.

parameters.name: requires_grad
parameters.description: Whether the tensor should track gradients from the start.

methods.name: from_numpy
methods.signature: int from_numpy(int arr, Device device, bool requires_grad)
methods.summary: Constructs a :class:`TensorImpl` by copying data from a NumPy array.
methods.extended:
The data is always copied into a fresh :class:`CpuStorage` buffer.
When ``device == Device::GPU`` the buffer is subsequently uploaded
to MLX via the GPU bridge, yielding a :class:`GpuStorage`-backed
tensor.

parameters.name: arr
parameters.description: Source NumPy array.  Must be a signed / floating / complex dtype.

parameters.name: device
parameters.description: Target device for the constructed tensor.

parameters.name: requires_grad
parameters.description: Whether the resulting tensor tracks gradients.

returns.description: Newly constructed tensor.

raises.description: The array has an unsigned dtype.  The caller must cast to a signed integer or floating-point type first.

methods.name: storage
methods.signature: const int & storage()
methods.summary: Returns a const reference to the backing :class:`Storage` variant.

methods.name: mutable_storage
methods.signature: int & mutable_storage()
methods.summary: Returns a mutable reference to the backing :class:`Storage` variant.
methods.extended:
Used by in-place ops; callers are responsible for bumping the version
counter when they mutate the underlying bytes.

methods.name: storage_offset
methods.signature: int storage_offset()
methods.summary: Returns the byte offset of this tensor's first element from the start of :func:`storage`.

returns.description: ``0`` for owning tensors; non-zero for views created with :func:`make_view`.

methods.name: storage_is_shared
methods.signature: bool storage_is_shared()
methods.summary: Predicate: is this tensor's CPU storage aliased by another tensor?

returns.description: ``true`` when the underlying :class:`CpuStorage` ``ptr`` is referenced by more than one ``shared_ptr`` — i.e. at least one other view of this tensor exists.  Always ``false`` for :class:`GpuStorage` and :class:`SharedStorage` variants (those variants do not currently report sharing through this hook).

methods.name: make_view
methods.signature: int make_view(const int & base, int shape, int stride, int offset_bytes)
methods.summary: Creates a new :class:`TensorImpl` that aliases ``base``'s :class:`Storage` with a different shape, stride, and optional byte offset.
methods.extended:
The view inherits ``base``'s ``requires_grad`` and ``is_leaf`` flags.
No data is copied — modifying either tensor through an in-place op
affects the other (which is precisely why the shared
:type:`VersionCounter` exists).

parameters.name: base
parameters.description: Tensor whose storage the view aliases.

parameters.name: stride
parameters.description: Byte-stride vector for the new view.  Need not be contiguous.

parameters.name: offset_bytes
parameters.description: Byte offset from ``base``'s storage origin.  Defaults to ``0``.

returns.description: New view tensor sharing ``base``'s storage.

methods.name: meta
methods.signature: const TensorMeta & meta()
methods.summary: Returns a const reference to the :class:`TensorMeta` bundle.

methods.name: shape
methods.signature: const int & shape()
methods.summary: Returns the shape vector.  Backs the Python ``.shape`` attribute.

methods.name: stride
methods.signature: const int & stride()
methods.summary: Returns the byte-stride vector.  Backs the Python ``.stride()`` method.

methods.name: dtype
methods.signature: Dtype dtype()
methods.summary: Returns the element dtype.  Backs the Python ``.dtype`` attribute.

methods.name: device
methods.signature: Device device()
methods.summary: Returns the device tag.  Backs the Python ``.device`` attribute.

methods.name: requires_grad
methods.signature: bool requires_grad()
methods.summary: Returns the current ``requires_grad`` flag.

returns.description: ``false`` when there is no :class:`AutogradMeta` at all (the common inference path — avoids an indirection).

methods.name: is_leaf
methods.signature: bool is_leaf()
methods.summary: Returns the leaf-status flag.
methods.extended:
A *leaf* tensor was constructed directly by the user; a non-leaf
tensor is the output of a differentiable op and carries a non-null
``grad_fn``.

returns.description: ``true`` for leaves, ``true`` (default) when there is no :class:`AutogradMeta` yet.

methods.name: version
methods.signature: int version()
methods.summary: Returns the autograd version counter.
methods.extended:
Bumped by :func:`bump_version` on every in-place mutation; autograd
snapshots this at forward time and checks it at backward time to
catch illegal mutations.

returns.description: Current version.  ``0`` when no :class:`AutogradMeta` exists.

methods.name: grad_fn
methods.signature: const int & grad_fn()
methods.summary: Returns the backward function pointer for this tensor.

returns.description: Non-null for non-leaf tensors produced by a differentiable op, ``nullptr`` for leaves and tensors with no :class:`AutogradMeta`.

methods.name: grad_output_nr
methods.signature: int grad_output_nr()
methods.summary: Returns the output index that connects this tensor to its :func:`grad_fn`.
methods.extended:
Multi-output ops produce several tensors that share one
:class:`Node`; this index tells the backward pass which of the
node's outputs the tensor corresponds to.

returns.description: Output index, or ``0`` when no :class:`AutogradMeta` exists.

methods.name: grad_storage
methods.signature: const int & grad_storage()
methods.summary: Returns the accumulated gradient :class:`Storage`, if any.

returns.description: :type:`std::nullopt` when no gradient has been accumulated, or when the tensor has no :class:`AutogradMeta` at all.

methods.name: mutable_grad_storage
methods.signature: int & mutable_grad_storage()
methods.summary: Returns a mutable reference to the gradient :class:`Storage`, constructing the :class:`AutogradMeta` on demand.
methods.extended: Used by the engine's gradient accumulation paths.

returns.description: Mutable reference; the optional may still be empty even though :class:`AutogradMeta` is now allocated.

methods.name: set_dtype
methods.signature: void set_dtype(Dtype dt)
methods.summary: Sets the dtype field.  Does not touch the underlying bytes; used by pure metadata reinterpretation paths.

methods.name: set_device
methods.signature: void set_device(Device dv)
methods.summary: Sets the device field.  Does not move data; used after an in-place device transfer has updated :attr:`storage_`.

methods.name: set_requires_grad
methods.signature: void set_requires_grad(bool v)
methods.summary: Enables or disables gradient tracking.
methods.extended:
Constructs :class:`AutogradMeta` lazily when ``v`` is ``true``.
Setting to ``false`` does not destroy an already-existing
:class:`AutogradMeta` — only the flag is cleared, so historical
version counters / saved tensors remain valid.

methods.name: set_leaf
methods.signature: void set_leaf(bool v)
methods.summary: Sets the leaf-status flag, constructing :class:`AutogradMeta` if it does not yet exist.

methods.name: set_grad_fn
methods.signature: void set_grad_fn(int fn)
methods.summary: Attaches the backward function pointer.
methods.extended:
Marks the tensor as non-leaf (callers usually pair this with
``set_leaf(false)``).

parameters.name: fn
parameters.description: Backward node produced by the forward-time op kernel.

methods.name: set_grad_output_nr
methods.signature: void set_grad_output_nr(int nr)
methods.summary: Sets the output index associated with :func:`grad_fn`.

parameters.name: nr
parameters.description: Zero-based output index within the producing :class:`Node`.

methods.name: clear_grad_fn
methods.signature: void clear_grad_fn()
methods.summary: Drops the backward function pointer without removing the :class:`AutogradMeta`.
methods.extended:
Used when an in-place op detaches a tensor from the autograd graph
(``tensor.detach_()``-style semantics).

methods.name: set_grad_storage
methods.signature: void set_grad_storage(int grad)
methods.summary: Stores a gradient :class:`Storage` directly.

parameters.name: grad
parameters.description: Gradient buffer to adopt.  Must have the same dtype / device / shape as this tensor.

methods.name: grad_as_impl
methods.signature: int grad_as_impl()
methods.summary: Returns the graph-mode gradient as a full :class:`TensorImpl`.
methods.extended:
Populated when ``backward(create_graph=True)`` was called — allowing
the gradient itself to participate in further autograd (Hessian-vector
products, MAML, second-order ops).

returns.description: The graph-mode gradient tensor, or ``nullptr`` when no graph-mode gradient has been accumulated.

methods.name: set_grad_impl
methods.signature: void set_grad_impl(int g)
methods.summary: Sets the graph-mode gradient tensor.

methods.name: accumulate_grad_impl
methods.signature: void accumulate_grad_impl(int g)
methods.summary: Accumulates a graph-mode gradient.
methods.extended:
First call sets the gradient outright; subsequent calls add ``g``
into the existing :func:`grad_as_impl` via the engine's ``add`` op
so the accumulation is differentiable when ``create_graph=true``.

methods.name: retains_grad
methods.signature: bool retains_grad()
methods.summary: Returns whether this tensor will retain its gradient even when it is a non-leaf.
methods.extended: Mirrors the reference framework's ``tensor.retain_grad()`` API.

returns.description: ``true`` when the engine should accumulate gradients into :attr:`grad_storage` for this tensor regardless of leaf status.

methods.name: set_retain_grad
methods.signature: void set_retain_grad(bool v)
methods.summary: Toggles gradient retention for non-leaf tensors.

methods.name: bump_version
methods.signature: void bump_version()
methods.summary: Increments the autograd version counter.
methods.extended:
Called by every in-place op so autograd can detect mutations of
tensors that were saved for backward.  No-op when no
:class:`AutogradMeta` exists — i.e. the tensor has never participated
in the autograd graph, so version tracking is unnecessary.

methods.name: numel
methods.signature: int numel()
methods.summary: Returns the total number of elements (product of shape dimensions).

returns.description: ``1`` for a scalar (empty shape).

methods.name: nbytes
methods.signature: int nbytes()
methods.summary: Returns the total byte footprint of the tensor's logical data.
methods.extended:
Equal to ``numel() * dtype_size(dtype())``; does **not** include any
extra storage capacity beyond the tensor's view.

returns.description: Logical byte size of the tensor data.

methods.name: is_contiguous
methods.signature: bool is_contiguous()
methods.summary: Predicate: does the current stride equal the row-major contiguous stride for this shape and dtype?

returns.description: ``true`` for canonically laid-out tensors; ``false`` for views that were transposed / sliced / otherwise reordered.

methods.name: data_as_python
methods.signature: int data_as_python()
methods.summary: Returns the tensor data as a NumPy array.
methods.extended:
For CPU tensors the returned array is a zero-copy view backed by a
:class:`py::capsule` that keeps the :class:`TensorImpl` alive.  For
GPU tensors the data is synchronously downloaded to a temporary CPU
buffer first.

**See Also**

:func:`to_bytes`  — NumPy-free alternative.
:func:`tolist`    — nested Python list alternative.

returns.description: A NumPy array (one of the supported dtypes, except for :attr:`Dtype::C64` which is represented as a complex64 array).

methods.name: grad_as_python
methods.signature: int grad_as_python()
methods.summary: Returns the accumulated gradient as a NumPy array.
methods.extended: Identical download semantics to :func:`data_as_python`.

returns.description: The gradient array, or :func:`py::none` when no gradient has been computed yet.

methods.name: to_bytes
methods.signature: int to_bytes()
methods.summary: Returns the tensor data as a contiguous bytes blob, in row-major order.
methods.extended:
GPU tensors are downloaded to a temporary CPU buffer first.  No NumPy
dependency, by design — this path is what the serialization layer and
``__repr__`` use to avoid pulling NumPy into ``import lucid``.

returns.description: Row-major bytes blob of length ``nbytes()``.

methods.name: from_bytes
methods.signature: int from_bytes(int data, int shape, Dtype dtype, Device device, bool requires_grad)
methods.summary: Reconstructs a :class:`TensorImpl` from a raw bytes blob + metadata.
methods.extended:
Inverse of :func:`to_bytes`.  When ``device == Device::GPU`` the
buffer is uploaded to MLX before returning.

parameters.name: data
parameters.description: Row-major byte payload.  Must be ``shape_numel(shape) * dtype_size(dtype)`` bytes long.

parameters.name: device
parameters.description: Target device.  GPU triggers an MLX upload.

parameters.name: requires_grad
parameters.description: Whether the reconstructed tensor tracks gradients.

returns.description: Newly constructed tensor.

methods.name: transfer_to_device
methods.signature: int transfer_to_device(Device target, bool requires_grad)
methods.summary: Cross-device copy without round-tripping through Python or NumPy.
methods.extended:
Dispatch summary:

* CPU → GPU: contiguous-snapshot the source view, then
    ``gpu::upload_cpu_to_gpu`` into a fresh GPU-private MLX array.
  * GPU → CPU: ``gpu::download_gpu_to_cpu`` into a fresh
    :class:`CpuStorage`.
  * :class:`SharedStorage` is handled by the separate
    ``transfer_storage`` API (zero-copy relabel); calling this method
    on a :class:`SharedStorage` tensor falls back to a contiguous CPU
    snapshot path.

parameters.name: requires_grad
parameters.description: Whether the resulting tensor tracks gradients.

returns.description: New tensor on ``target`` with the same shape and dtype.

methods.name: tolist
methods.signature: int tolist()
methods.summary: Converts the tensor to a nested Python list (or a Python scalar for 0-d tensors).
methods.extended:
NumPy-free — mirrors :func:`item`'s dtype dispatch but walks the full
shape.  Handles every supported dtype including :attr:`Dtype::C64`
(yields Python ``complex``), so callers never need to fall back to
the NumPy bridge.  GPU tensors are downloaded to a temporary
:class:`CpuStorage` via the :func:`to_bytes` snapshot path; the heavy
lifting then runs against a contiguous row-major byte buffer.

returns.description: Nested ``list`` of Python scalars, or a single scalar when the tensor is 0-d.

methods.name: item
methods.signature: int item()
methods.summary: Extracts a single-element tensor's value as a Python scalar.
methods.extended:
GPU tensors are downloaded to CPU first.  The :attr:`Dtype::F16`
IEEE-754 binary16 → ``float`` conversion is performed engine-side so
the Python wrapper avoids duplicating the bit-fiddling logic from
:func:`to_string`.

returns.description: ``int`` / ``float`` / ``bool`` / ``complex`` depending on dtype.

methods.name: to_string
methods.signature: int to_string(int precision, int threshold, int edgeitems)
methods.summary: Renders the tensor data as a human-readable string suitable for ``__repr__``.
methods.extended:
The format roughly mirrors NumPy's ``array2string`` defaults but is
implemented entirely engine-side so neither Lucid nor its consumers
need NumPy.

parameters.name: precision
parameters.description: Significant digits for floating-point output.  Defaults to ``4``.

parameters.name: threshold
parameters.description: If ``numel > threshold``, an edge-summary is rendered instead of the full data.  Defaults to ``1000``.

parameters.name: edgeitems
parameters.description: How many items to keep at each edge of a truncated axis. Defaults to ``3``.

returns.description: Human-readable rendering of the tensor.

methods.name: grad_to_tensor
methods.signature: int grad_to_tensor()
methods.summary: Wraps the tensor's accumulated gradient as a fresh :class:`TensorImpl` that shares the underlying :class:`Storage`.
methods.extended:
Prefers the graph-mode :attr:`grad_impl` when present; otherwise wraps
the standard :attr:`grad` :class:`Storage` directly.  Replaces the
older NumPy round-trip used by the Python-side ``Tensor.grad``
accessor.

returns.description: Tensor view of the gradient, or ``nullptr`` when no gradient has been accumulated.

methods.name: copy_from
methods.signature: void copy_from(const TensorImpl & other)
methods.summary: Copies data from ``other`` into this tensor.
methods.extended:
Supports :class:`CpuStorage`↔:class:`CpuStorage`,
:class:`GpuStorage`↔:class:`GpuStorage`, and all combinations
involving :class:`SharedStorage`.  Bumps the version counter on
success.

parameters.name: other
parameters.description: Source tensor.  Must have identical device, dtype, and shape.

raises.description: ``other.device() != device()``.
``other.dtype() != dtype()``.
``other.shape() != shape()``.

methods.name: zero_grad
methods.signature: void zero_grad()
methods.summary: Clears the accumulated gradient.
methods.extended:
Sets :attr:`grad` to :type:`std::nullopt` but leaves the
:class:`AutogradMeta` itself in place so subsequent backward passes
continue to accumulate.

methods.name: eval
methods.signature: void eval()
methods.summary: Forces evaluation of this tensor's lazy MLX computation graph.
methods.extended:
GPU tensors: calls ``mlx::core::eval()`` on the underlying array,
materialising the buffer.
CPU tensors: no-op (CPU storage is always materialised).
notes:
Required before any path that wants to dereference the GPU buffer
through a CPU pointer (e.g. :func:`to_bytes`, :func:`data_as_python`).

methods.name: ensure_autograd
methods.signature: AutogradMeta * ensure_autograd()
methods.summary: Returns a pointer to the AutogradMeta, constructing it in-place if it does not yet exist.  The returned pointer is stable (optional stores in-place) as long as autograd_ is not reset.

methods.name: shape_
methods.signature: int & shape_()
methods.summary: Private mutable references to the meta fields — used only within TensorImpl's own methods; callers should use the public set_* functions.

methods.name: stride_
methods.signature: int & stride_()
methods.summary: Mutable stride accessor — returns the underlying ``Stride`` by reference so engine-side ops can rewrite strides in place without going through the full ``set_meta`` path.  Pairs with the public const :func:`stride`.
notes:
Modifying the stride WITHOUT also touching ``storage_`` or ``shape_``
produces an invalid TensorImpl — only call this from internal kernel
code that holds the necessary invariants.  Does NOT bump the version
counter; callers that change tensor geometry through this hook are
responsible for whatever bookkeeping autograd requires.

methods.name: dtype_field
methods.signature: Dtype & dtype_field()
methods.summary: Mutable dtype accessor — returns the underlying ``Dtype`` by reference. Rarely used outside engine internals; pairs with the public const :func:`dtype`.
notes:
Changing the dtype WITHOUT also reinterpreting / reallocating
``storage_`` is a footgun: the bytes on disk no longer match the
declared element type, so subsequent ops will read garbage.  Only call
from paths that have already updated storage to match the new dtype.

methods.name: device_field
methods.signature: Device & device_field()
methods.summary: Mutable device accessor — returns the underlying ``Device`` by reference.  Rarely used outside engine internals; pairs with the public const :func:`device`.
notes:
Same footgun as :func:`dtype_field`: changing the device WITHOUT also
migrating ``storage_`` to the new device produces a TensorImpl that
claims to live on one stream while its bytes live on another.  Only
call from paths that have already moved the storage.

members.summary: Reference-counted tensor implementation owned by every Python-level :class:`lucid.Tensor`.
members.extended:
The C++ op layer receives and returns :type:`TensorImplPtr`
(``shared_ptr<TensorImpl>``) directly; the Python wrapper is a thin layer
that forwards attribute access here.  :class:`TensorImpl` extends
:class:`std::enable_shared_from_this` so member functions can hand out
additional ``shared_ptr`` references to ``*this`` safely (e.g. when
building autograd graph nodes that capture the producing tensor).

**See Also**

:class:`Storage`        — backing memory variant.
:class:`TensorMeta`     — shape / stride / dtype / device bundle.
:class:`AutogradMeta`   — autograd bookkeeping.

attributes.name: storage
attributes.description: Backing memory variant — :class:`CpuStorage`, :class:`GpuStorage`, or :class:`SharedStorage`.  Shared with every view.

attributes.name: offset
attributes.description: Byte offset of this tensor's first element from the start of :attr:`storage`.  Non-zero only for views created by :func:`make_view`.

attributes.name: meta
attributes.description: Shape, stride, dtype, and device.  Views carry their own :class:`TensorMeta` independent of the base.

attributes.name: autograd
attributes.description: Autograd bookkeeping (``requires_grad``, ``is_leaf``, version, ``grad_fn``, accumulated gradient).  ``std::nullopt`` for tensors that have never participated in the autograd graph.

members.name: TensorMeta
members.signature: TensorMeta

methods.name: TensorMeta
methods.signature: void TensorMeta()
methods.summary: Default-construct an empty descriptor with zero-length shape and stride, dtype :attr:`F32`, and device :attr:`CPU`.
methods.extended:
Useful as a placeholder when a :class:`TensorMeta` will be filled
in by a later assignment (e.g. inside output-shape inference
loops that materialise one meta per op output).
notes:
The default-constructed instance describes a 0-dim scalar of
dtype ``F32`` on the CPU; do not assume the shape vector has any
particular capacity reserved.

methods.name: numel
methods.signature: int numel()
methods.summary: Total number of logical elements described by :attr:`shape`.
methods.extended:
Computed as the product of all dimension sizes; a 0-dim scalar
(empty ``shape``) returns ``1`` by convention.

**Math**

$$ \mathrm{numel} = \prod_{i=0}^{N-1} \mathrm{shape}_i $$

**See Also**

:func:`shape_numel` — the underlying element-count helper.
:meth:`nbytes` — contiguous byte count for the same shape/dtype.

returns.description: ``shape[0] * shape[1] * ... * shape[N-1]``, or ``1`` for a scalar.

notes:
Delegates to :func:`shape_numel` from :file:`Shape.h`, which
handles unresolved negative dimensions (returns ``0``) gracefully.

methods.name: nbytes
methods.signature: int nbytes()
methods.summary: Total bytes occupied by a contiguous layout of :attr:`shape` and :attr:`dtype`.
methods.extended:
Equal to ``numel() * dtype_size(dtype)``; does NOT inspect
:attr:`stride`, so non-contiguous descriptors still report the
size of the *equivalent contiguous* tensor, not their actual
memory footprint in the parent storage.

**Math**

$$ \mathrm{nbytes} = \mathrm{numel} \cdot \mathrm{sizeof}(\mathrm{dtype}) $$

**See Also**

:meth:`numel`     — logical element count.
:func:`dtype_size` — width of a single element.

returns.description: Number of bytes required to hold ``numel()`` elements of :attr:`dtype`.

methods.name: is_contiguous
methods.signature: bool is_contiguous()
methods.summary: Whether the stored :attr:`stride` matches the reference contiguous (row-major) stride for :attr:`shape` and :attr:`dtype`.
methods.extended:
A tensor is C-contiguous when, for every axis ``i``,
``stride[i] == dtype_size(dtype) * prod(shape[i+1:])``.  This
method delegates the layout check to
:func:`contiguous_stride` and compares the two ``Stride``
vectors element-wise.

**See Also**

:func:`contiguous_stride` — produces the reference stride.
:meth:`lucid::core::TensorMeta::is_contiguous_for` — engine-side
    equivalent that takes an explicit ``elem_size``.

returns.description: ``true`` if :attr:`stride` equals ``contiguous_stride(shape, dtype_size(dtype))``, ``false`` otherwise.

notes:
Used by op kernels that require contiguous input to decide
whether to materialise the descriptor via a copy before
dispatch.  A default-constructed (empty-shape, empty-stride)
descriptor is contiguous by definition because both vectors
compare equal.

members.summary: Pure metadata descriptor for a tensor — bundles shape, stride, dtype, and device without owning any :class:`Storage`.
members.extended:
Used wherever the engine needs to reason about a tensor's geometry
without touching its data: broadcast resolution, view / reshape
planning, output-shape inference for op dispatch, and signature
hashing for kernel caches.  Cheap to copy (a handful of small
``std::vector`` handles plus two enum ints), so callers pass it by
value freely.

Strides follow the BYTE-OFFSET convention — ``stride[i]`` is the
byte delta required to step one element along axis ``i``.  A
:class:`TensorMeta` is considered contiguous when its strides match
:func:`contiguous_stride(shape, dtype_size(dtype))`.

**See Also**

:class:`lucid::core::TensorMeta` — engine-internal twin embedded in
    :class:`TensorImpl`.
:func:`contiguous_stride` — canonical stride producer.
notes:
All constructors are deliberately non-explicit to allow
aggregate-style initialisation in shape-inference helpers
(``TensorMeta out{shape, dtype, device};``).

attributes.name: shape
attributes.description: Per-dimension sizes.  An empty shape denotes a 0-dim scalar.

attributes.name: stride
attributes.description: Per-dimension byte strides.  Length must equal ``shape.size()`` for the layout to be considered well-formed; the default constructor leaves both vectors empty.

attributes.name: dtype
attributes.description: Element type tag.  Defaults to :attr:`Dtype::F32`.

attributes.name: device
attributes.description: Memory domain the descriptor refers to (CPU = Accelerate, GPU = MLX).  Defaults to :attr:`Device::CPU`.

members.name: UnaryKernel
members.signature: UnaryKernel

methods.name: grad_formula_impl
methods.signature: int grad_formula_impl(const int &, const int &, const int &)
methods.summary: Default graph-mode gradient formula — concrete ops override.

parameters.name: g
parameters.description: Gradient of the loss with respect to this op's output, as a :class:`TensorImpl` that itself carries ``grad_fn`` so the backward computation is differentiable.

parameters.name: a
parameters.description: The saved forward input (possibly broadcast / cast to the effective dtype).

parameters.name: out
parameters.description: The saved forward output (populated only when ``kSavesOutput == true``).

returns.description: ``grad_input`` as a fully-traced :class:`TensorImpl`.

raises.description: The default base implementation always throws.  Concrete ops must override to support ``create_graph=True``.

methods.name: name
methods.signature: int name()
methods.summary: Return the canonical schema name of the concrete op.

methods.name: node_name
methods.signature: int node_name()
methods.summary: Return the autograd node label (same string as :meth:`name`).

methods.name: forward
methods.signature: int forward(const int & a)
methods.summary: Typed forward trampoline for a single-input op.

returns.description: The output tensor.  If ``a`` requires a gradient (and grad mode is enabled) the result has its ``grad_fn`` set to a freshly constructed ``Derived`` backward node.

raises.description: If the schema's shape contract is violated.
If the input dtype is not compatible with the schema's accepted dtype set.

notes: Behaviour

1. Validate ``a`` is non-null.
  2. Resolve the effective dtype via :class:`SchemaGuard`.
  3. Materialise contiguous on CPU when ``a`` is non-contiguous.
  4. Cast to the effective dtype if needed.
  5. Dispatch to ``dispatch`` / ``gpu_kernel`` / ``cpu_kernel``
     (see file header for priority).
  6. Wire the autograd graph when ``kHasGradient`` and any input
     requires a gradient.

methods.name: apply
methods.signature: int apply(int grad_out)
methods.summary: Backward implementation invoked by the autograd engine.

parameters.name: grad_out
parameters.description: The upstream gradient with respect to this op's output.

returns.description: Single-element vector containing ``grad_input`` reduced back to ``input_shapes_[0]`` via :func:`reduce_grad_to_shape`.

notes:
The reduction step is needed when the forward broadcast the
input to a larger output shape; for purely shape-preserving
unary ops it is a no-op.

methods.name: apply_for_graph
methods.signature: int apply_for_graph(const int & grad_out)
methods.summary: Graph-mode backward — supports create_graph=True.

parameters.name: grad_out
parameters.description: The upstream gradient, retained as a :class:`TensorImpl` with its own ``grad_fn`` for higher-order differentiation.

returns.description: Single-element vector containing the fully-traced ``grad_input`` reduced back to the original input shape via :func:`sum_op` / :func:`reshape_op`.

raises.description: If the saved input pointer was not captured (i.e. the forward ran without create_graph=True), or if the concrete op did not override :meth:`grad_formula_impl`.

members.summary: CRTP base for single-input, single-output op kernels.
members.extended:
Inherits :class:`AutogradNode\<Derived, 1\>` (one saved input slot)
and :class:`kernel::IKernel` so an instance can be held polymorphically
while still exposing the typed static ``forward()`` trampoline.

Concrete ops declare themselves as
``class FooBackward : public UnaryKernel<FooBackward>`` and provide:

- ``static constexpr OpSchema schema_v1`` — op name + AMP policy.
  - ``static cpu_kernel(a, out_shape, dtype) -> CpuStorage`` and/or
    ``static gpu_kernel(a, out_shape, dtype) -> GpuStorage``, **or**
    ``static dispatch(IBackend&, a, out_shape, dtype) -> Storage``.
  - ``Storage grad_formula(Storage grad_out)`` — the local Jacobian
    product computed at backward time.
  - Optional ``grad_formula_impl(grad_out, a_impl, out_impl) -> TensorImplPtr``
    for graph-mode (create_graph=True) higher-order differentiation.

Template Parameters
-------------------
Derived : class
    The concrete CRTP self-type.

**See Also**

:class:`BinaryKernel`, :class:`NaryKernel`, :class:`VariadicKernel`.
:class:`IKernel` — the abstract base above the CRTP layer.
notes:
AMP policy and dtype promotion are forwarded through
:class:`SchemaGuard` using ``Derived::schema_v1``; the effective dtype
returned drives both the input cast and the saved-input dtype.

attributes.name: kSavesInput
attributes.description: Whether ``forward()`` snapshots ``a->storage()`` into ``saved_inputs_[0]`` for use in ``grad_formula``.  Defaults to ``true``; set ``false`` when the gradient formula uses only the saved output (e.g. :class:`ReLU` keyed on output sign).

attributes.name: kSavesOutput
attributes.description: Whether ``forward()`` additionally snapshots the output storage into ``saved_output_``.  Defaults to ``false``.  Set ``true`` for ops whose backward is cheap in terms of ``y`` (e.g. :class:`Exp`, :class:`Sigmoid`).

attributes.name: kHasGradient
attributes.description: Whether the op participates in autograd.  Defaults to ``true``; set ``false`` for non-differentiable ops to skip all graph wiring.

methods.name: forward
methods.signature: int forward(const int & x, const int & kernel, const int & stride, const int & pad, const int & dilation)
methods.summary: Run the forward im2col extraction.

parameters.name: x
parameters.description: Input of shape ``(B, C, S_0, ..., S_{N-1})`` where ``N = kernel.size()``.

returns.description: Column matrix of shape ``(B, C * prod(K), prod(O))``.

raises.description: If the input rank disagrees with ``N + 2`` or any output extent is non-positive.

methods.name: apply
methods.signature: int apply(int grad_out)
methods.summary: Backward — the fold (col2im) operation that scatters the patches back to spatial positions, summing overlaps.

parameters.name: grad_out
parameters.description: Upstream gradient shaped like the forward output.

returns.description: Single-element vector containing ``dx`` with the original input shape.

members.summary: Autograd node for the Unfold (im2col) operation.
members.extended:
Slides a kernel-sized window over each spatial dimension of the input
and lays the resulting patches out as columns of a 3-D matrix:

$$
y[b,\, c \cdot \prod K + k_0 \cdot K_1 \cdots + k_{N-1},\,
  o_0 \cdot \prod O' + \cdots + o_{N-1}]
  = x[b,\, c,\, s_0 o_0 + d_0 k_0,\, \ldots].
$$

The output column count equals $\prod_i O_i$, the same value a
matching convolution would produce.  Supports $N \in \{1, 2, 3\}$
inferred from ``kernel.size()``.

attributes.description: Registered op (``"unfold"``, ``AmpPolicy::KeepInput``).

attributes.name: kernel_
attributes.description: Kernel sizes per spatial axis.

attributes.name: stride_
attributes.description: Strides per spatial axis.

attributes.name: pad_
attributes.description: Zero-padding per spatial axis.

attributes.name: dilation_
attributes.description: Dilations per spatial axis.

methods.name: pair
methods.signature: Pair pair(const int & a, const int & b, int op_name)
methods.summary: Builds a two-tensor validator.

methods.name: Validator
methods.signature: void Validator(const int & t, int label)
methods.summary: Construct a ``Validator`` bound to ``t`` with the given ``label``.
methods.extended:
Private because callers must go through the :cpp:func:`input`
factory.  Stores ``t`` by reference (not copy), so the resulting
validator must live no longer than the caller's ``TensorImplPtr``.
``label`` is woven into every error message the chain emits, so
callers should pass the parameter's public name (e.g. ``"weight"``).

members.summary: Fluent precondition checker for a single tensor input.
members.extended:
Each method either evaluates one precondition and returns ``*this``
(success), or throws the appropriate typed :class:`LucidError` subclass
via :class:`ErrorBuilder` (failure).  Chaining lets op implementations
state all of their input requirements in one compact expression at the
top of their body.

Instances cannot be constructed directly — use the static
:cpp:func:`input` factory.  The validator holds a *reference* to the
caller's ``TensorImplPtr`` rather than a copy, so the chain must live no
longer than the tensor it inspects (this is the normal case when the
chain appears on a single statement at the op's entry point).

**See Also**

:class:`Validator::Pair` : Two-tensor sibling for cross-tensor checks.
:class:`ErrorBuilder` : Underlying throw machinery.
examples:
```
Validator::input(t, "linear.weight")
    .non_null()
    .float_only()
    .ndim(2);
```

attributes.name: t_
attributes.description: Reference to the tensor being checked.  Lifetime is the caller's responsibility.

attributes.name: label_
attributes.description: Human-readable identifier used as the op-name argument to :class:`ErrorBuilder` so error messages name the offending tensor (e.g. ``"linear.weight"``).

Mirrors :class:`Validator` but for checks that compare two operands
(same dtype, same device, same shape, etc.).  Constructed via the
static :cpp:func:`Validator::pair` factory — direct construction
exists only so the factory can return by value.
examples:
```
Validator::pair(a, b, "matmul")
    .both_non_null()
    .same_dtype()

```

members.name: VersionMismatch
members.signature: VersionMismatch

methods.name: VersionMismatch
methods.signature: void VersionMismatch(int expected, int got, int context)
methods.summary: Construct a ``VersionMismatch`` with the version captured at forward time vs the version observed at backward time, plus the op-level context annotation the call site captured.  Raised when a saved tensor was mutated in-place between forward and backward.

methods.name: expected_version
methods.signature: int expected_version()
methods.summary: Returns the version observed when the tensor was saved.

returns.description: Forward-pass version captured at save time.

methods.name: got_version
methods.signature: int got_version()
methods.summary: Returns the version observed during the backward pass.

returns.description: Current version of the tensor when the saved snapshot was restored.

members.summary: Thrown by the autograd engine when a saved tensor is mutated between the forward and backward pass.
members.extended:
Each tensor carries a monotonically increasing version counter; the saved
snapshot records the version observed at save time.  When the snapshot is
restored during backward, the engine compares the saved version against
the tensor's current version and raises :class:`VersionMismatch` if they
differ — preventing silently incorrect gradients caused by in-place
mutation.  Mapped to ``lucid._C.engine.VersionMismatch`` on the Python
side.

parameters.name: expected
parameters.description: Version that was captured during the forward pass.

parameters.name: got
parameters.description: Version observed during the backward pass.

parameters.name: context
parameters.description: Annotation produced by :class:`ErrorBuilder` (typically the saving op's name).

notes:
In-place ops on tensors that participate in autograd should either bump
the version counter on the underlying storage or be re-implemented as a
non-aliasing copy.  Catching :class:`VersionMismatch` and continuing is
strongly discouraged — the gradient is no longer well-defined.

attributes.name: expected_
attributes.description: Stored snapshot version.

attributes.name: got_
attributes.description: Stored current version.
