methods.summary: Graph-mode: reshape via reshape_op so the result is tracked.

methods.name: node_name
methods.signature: int node_name()
methods.summary: Graph label — ``"reshape"`` — for debug printing and profiler traces.  Overrides :func:`Node::node_name`.

members.summary: Autograd node for reshape, squeeze, and unsqueeze.
members.extended:
Forward reinterprets the flat element buffer under a new shape.  The
backend dispatcher's reshape creates a new ``Storage`` alias over the same
physical buffer; no data movement occurs.  Backward reshapes the incoming
gradient back to the original input shape recorded in ``input_shapes_[0]``;
because reshape is its own inverse on contiguous buffers (element ordering
is unchanged), the backward pass is simply another reshape call.

**Math**

$$ y_k = x_k \quad \text{for } k \in [0, \mathrm{numel}(x)), $$
where indices are taken in row-major flat ordering.  The mapping between
multi-dim indices changes, but the linear element ordering does not.

**Shape**

``input_shapes_[0]`` — shape of the input tensor before the reshape.
``out_shape_``       — shape after the reshape (inherited from
                       ``FuncOp``).  Both must have identical element
                       counts.

**See Also**

:func:`reshape_op`, :func:`squeeze_op`, :func:`unsqueeze_op`,
:func:`contiguous_op`.

parameters.name: grad_out
parameters.description: Incoming gradient, shaped according to the forward output (``out_shape_``).

returns.description: Single-element vector containing the gradient reshaped to ``input_shapes_[0]``.

notes:
In graph mode ``apply_for_graph`` routes through :func:`reshape_op` so the
reshape itself becomes a tracked node and the upstream gradient flow
participates in graph optimisation.

attributes.name: schema_v1
attributes.description: ``"view"``, ``AmpPolicy::KeepInput``, requires_grad=true, is_view=true.

members.name: AutocastGuard
members.signature: AutocastGuard

methods.name: AutocastGuard
methods.signature: void AutocastGuard(Dtype target)
methods.summary: Activates AMP with ``target`` as the effective compute dtype.
methods.extended:
Saves the previous ``(active, dtype)`` pair so the destructor can
restore it.  Does not validate ``target`` — any :class:`Dtype` is
accepted (the per-op :class:`AmpPolicy` controls how that dtype is
interpreted at dispatch time).

parameters.name: target
parameters.description: The autocast target dtype reported by :func:`amp::active_dtype` while this guard is live.  Typical values are :class:`Dtype::F16` for GPU half-precision training and :class:`Dtype::BF16` for bfloat16 training; :class:`Dtype::F32` produces an effectively neutral guard.

methods.name: ~AutocastGuard
methods.signature: void ~AutocastGuard()
methods.summary: Restores the previous AMP state captured at construction.
methods.extended: Runs unconditionally — required for correct nesting semantics.

methods.name: operator=
methods.signature: AutocastGuard & operator=(const AutocastGuard &)

members.summary: RAII guard that activates AMP with a target dtype for the duration of the guard's lifetime.
members.extended:
Construction sets the thread-local autocast state to ``(active=true,
dtype=target)`` and saves the previous values.  Destruction restores
the previous values unconditionally, so guards may be nested freely
(an inner ``ForceFP32`` scope inside an outer ``F16`` scope, etc.).

The Python-side ``lucid.amp.autocast`` context manager wraps this guard
and is the user-facing entry point — direct C++ use is rare outside
the engine itself.
examples: .. code-block:: cpp
notes:
Copy and move are deleted: this is a stack-only RAII object, and
duplicating it would corrupt the saved/restore state machine.

The Python binding exposes ``__enter__`` / ``__exit__`` semantics
without restoring on exit (the Python ``autocast`` class implements
proper RAII in Python); the destructor still runs the restore when
the C++ object is finally collected.

members.name: ConvOpts
members.signature: ConvOpts
members.summary: Parameter bag for a convolution op (legacy compatibility path).
members.extended:
Bundles batch / channel counts together with shape, stride, padding,
dilation, and grouping for an N-D convolution.  The primary
convolution code paths now use :class:`IBackend::ConvNdOpts` plus
explicit dimension arguments; this struct is retained for older
callers that build a free-standing ``ConvOpts`` value.

**See Also**

:class:`IBackend::ConvNdOpts` — modern, fixed-size parameter bag.

methods.name: astype
methods.signature: int astype(const int & a, const int & shape, Dtype src_dt, Dtype dst_dt)
methods.extended:
Generic Cartesian-product cast over the supported scalar dtypes.  Earlier
versions of this file hand-spelled every supported (src, dst) pair, which
left holes (notably ``Bool -> I64``) that surfaced during the 3.0.2 +
followup smoke runs — bool reductions need to promote through I64 first
and that promotion was silently NotImplementedError.  The dispatch below
covers every pair among {F16, F32, F64, I8, I16, I32, I64, Bool}.  C64
and BF16 are not handled here because their host representation isn't a
simple ``static_cast``-able scalar (F16 uses the IEEE half decoder from
``item()`` indirectly via the F32 path — handled with a small helper).

members.summary: CPU (Apple Accelerate-backed) concrete :class:`IBackend`.
members.extended:
Every public method satisfies the :class:`IBackend` contract; the
private section holds low-level type-dispatch helpers (``unary_op``,
``binary_op``, ``reduce_axes``) plus per-op compute routines that
delegate to the Accelerate helpers in :file:`Blas.h`,
:file:`Lapack.h`, :file:`Norm.h`, :file:`Pool.h`, :file:`Reduce.h`,
:file:`Vdsp.h`, and :file:`Vforce.h`.

**See Also**

:class:`IBackend` — abstract base.
:class:`GpuBackend` — MLX/Metal counterpart.
:class:`Dispatcher` — owns the singleton instance.
notes:
Marked ``final`` — there is exactly one CPU backend at runtime,
installed by :class:`CpuBackendRegistrar` at static-init time.

members.name: Dispatcher
members.signature: Dispatcher

methods.name: for_device
methods.signature: IBackend & for_device(Device d)
methods.summary: Returns the backend registered for device ``d``.
methods.extended:
The returned reference is non-owning and remains valid for the
entire process lifetime; callers must not delete it.

**See Also**

:func:`register_backend` — installer counterpart.

parameters.name: d
parameters.description: Device tag selecting which backend to return (CPU or GPU).

returns.description: Reference to the singleton backend instance for ``d``.

notes:
Behaviour is undefined if no backend has been registered for ``d``
— :func:`register_backend` must have been called for that device
before any tensor compute occurs.  ``noexcept``; safe to call
concurrently after initialisation.

methods.name: register_backend
methods.signature: void register_backend(Device d, int be)
methods.summary: Registers (or replaces) the backend for device ``d``.
methods.extended:
Transfers ownership of ``be`` into the dispatcher.  Any backend
previously stored at that slot is destroyed.

parameters.name: d
parameters.description: Device tag identifying which slot to populate.

parameters.name: be
parameters.description: Owning pointer to the concrete backend instance to install.

notes:
Called exactly once per :class:`Device` during process
initialisation (typically from :func:`CpuBackend::register_self`
and :func:`GpuBackend::register_self`, invoked by
``BackendInit.cpp``).  Not thread-safe — must complete before any
worker threads start issuing :func:`for_device` calls.

methods.name: instance
methods.signature: Dispatcher & instance()
methods.summary: Returns the Meyers-singleton dispatcher instance.
methods.extended:
Constructed on first call and destroyed at program exit per the
standard C++11 magic-statics guarantee.

returns.description: The process-wide dispatcher.

members.summary: Routes compute operations to the backend registered for a given Device.
members.extended:
Holds one :class:`IBackend` slot per :class:`Device` variant (currently
CPU and GPU).  :func:`register_backend` transfers ownership via
``std::unique_ptr``; :func:`for_device` returns a non-owning reference
valid for the lifetime of the process.  Every higher-level op in the
Lucid engine fetches its backend through this class — the dispatcher is
the single seam between device-agnostic op code and device-specific
compute libraries.

**See Also**

:class:`IBackend` — abstract per-device interface.
:func:`CpuBackend::register_self` — installs the CPU backend.
:func:`GpuBackend::register_self` — installs the GPU backend.
notes:
Thread safety: :func:`register_backend` is called exactly once per
device at process startup, before any worker threads exist.  After
that, :func:`for_device` is read-only and safe to call concurrently
from any thread.

The internal storage is a fixed-size ``std::array`` indexed by the
:class:`Device` enum value; ``kNumDevices`` must match the number of
:class:`Device` variants (currently ``CPU=0``, ``GPU=1``).

members.signature: GpuBackend

methods.summary: Construct + register a fresh :class:`GpuBackend` singleton with the Dispatcher.
methods.extended:

registration for ``Device::GPU``.  Normally invoked exactly once
by :struct:`GpuBackendRegistrar` at process start.

methods.name: silu
methods.signature: int silu(const int & a, const int & shape, Dtype dt)
methods.extended:
**Math**

$y = x \cdot \sigma(x)$ where $\sigma$ is the logistic sigmoid.

**See Also**

:meth:`silu_backward`.

methods.name: silu_backward
methods.signature: int silu_backward(const int & a, const int & grad, const int & shape, Dtype dt)
methods.summary: SiLU / Swish backward.
methods.extended:
Dispatches to :func:`gpu::mps::silu_backward` when policy allows
(universal dispatch — see :func:`should_dispatch_silu_backward`);
otherwise computes the gradient as a single fused MLX expression.

**Math**

$\partial y/\partial x = \sigma(x) \, (1 + x \, (1 - \sigma(x)))$.

methods.name: gelu
methods.signature: int gelu(const int & a, const int & shape, Dtype dt)
methods.summary: GELU forward (tanh approximation).
methods.extended:
**Math**

$y = 0.5 \, x \, (1 + \tanh[c_1 \, (x + c_2 x^3)])$ where
$c_1 = \sqrt{2/\pi}$, $c_2 = 0.044715$.

**See Also**

:meth:`gelu_backward`, :meth:`gelu_exact`.
notes:
Dispatches to :func:`gpu::mps::gelu_forward` when the per-op
policy allows; otherwise expands to the 7-op MLX composition
(kept bit-for-bit identical for parity).

methods.name: gelu_exact
methods.signature: int gelu_exact(const int & a, const int & shape, Dtype dt)
methods.summary: GELU exact (Gaussian-CDF) forward.
methods.extended:
**Math**

$y = 0.5 \, x \, (1 + \operatorname{erf}(x / \sqrt{2}))$.
notes:
Dispatches to :func:`gpu::mps::gelu_exact_forward` when policy
allows; otherwise composes via ``mlx::core::erf``.

methods.name: softmax
methods.signature: int softmax(const int & a, const int &, int axis, Dtype dt)
methods.summary: PERF: softmax + log_softmax produce contiguous output; their backwards are pure elementwise composition.  Dropping the contiguous() wraps lets downstream loss ops (cross_entropy_loss is the typical consumer) fuse into a single MLX kernel. ── Softmax family ──────────────────────────────────────────────────── Softmax along ``axis``.  Delegates to ``mlx::core::softmax``.
methods.extended:
**Math**

$y_i = e^{x_i} / \sum_j e^{x_j}$ along the chosen axis.

methods.name: softmax_backward
methods.signature: int softmax_backward(const int & z, const int & grad_out, const int & shape_param, int axis, Dtype dt)
methods.summary: Softmax backward, given the saved forward output ``z``.
methods.extended:
Dispatches to :func:`gpu::mps::softmax_backward` for large
reduction axes (≥1024 by default); otherwise composes
``z * (grad - sum(z * grad, axis))`` via MLX.

**Math**

$\partial L / \partial x_i = z_i \, (g_i - \sum_j z_j g_j)$.

methods.name: axis_scatter_via_multiaxis
methods.signature: int axis_scatter_via_multiaxis()
methods.summary: Multi-axis scatter where K = ndim and one coord array per axis. For non-scatter axis a: coord = arange(idx_shape[a]).reshape(1..N..1). For scatter axis dim: coord = idx_in (negative-normalised). Updates then has shape == idx_shape (same as the broadcast index shape), satisfying MLX's "updates.ndim == K + (ndim - K)" invariant.

methods.summary: Sliding-window unfold along ``dim`` producing rank+1 windows of ``size``.
methods.extended:
**Math**

Output dim ``L = (dim_size - size) / step + 1``.  Indices built
as ``starts[l] = l * step``, ``offsets[s] = s``, then gathered.

methods.name: matmul
methods.signature: int matmul(const int & a, const int & b, const MatmulOpts & opts, Dtype dt)
methods.summary: ── Linear algebra ──────────────────────────────────────────────────── General matrix multiply with optional broadcasted batch dims.

parameters.name: a, b
parameters.description: GPU tensors of compatible shapes.  Either side may be transposed via ``opts``.

parameters.name: dt
parameters.description: Output dtype.  Must be a real / complex floating type.

returns.description: Result of ``a @ b`` (possibly batched), wrapped as ``GpuStorage``.

notes:
Calls ``mlx::core::matmul``.  Uses Metal's BLAS-equivalent
tiled kernel internally; for very small matrices the dispatch
overhead may dominate.

methods.name: linear
methods.signature: int linear(const int & x, const int & weight, const int & bias, const int &, const int &, const int & out_shape, Dtype dt)
methods.summary: Fused affine: ``y = x @ W^T + b`` (the canonical ``nn.Linear`` op).
methods.extended:
**Math**

$y_{i,j} = \sum_k x_{i,k} W_{j,k} + b_j$.
notes:
Uses ``mlx::core::addmm`` when ``b`` is present so the multiply +
add fuse inside the MLX kernel; falls back to a separate ``matmul``
when ``b`` is null.  Deferred contiguous wrap matches the conv2d
path (cf perf-conv2d-deferred-contig).

methods.signature: StoragePair rms_norm_backward(const int & x, const int & gamma, const int & saved_rstd, const int & grad, int outer, int normalized_size, const int & x_shape, const int & gamma_shape, Dtype dt)

methods.name: layer_norm_forward
methods.signature: int layer_norm_forward(const int & x, const int & gamma, const int & beta, int outer, int normalized_size, double eps, const int & x_shape, Dtype dt)
methods.summary: ── Normalisation ───────────────────────────────────────────────────── LayerNorm forward via MLX's fused ``fast::layer_norm`` kernel.
methods.extended:
**Math**

$$\mu = \mathbb{E}[x], \quad \mathrm{rstd} = (\operatorname{Var}[x] + \varepsilon)^{-1/2}$$
$$y = \gamma \, (x - \mu) \, \mathrm{rstd} + \beta$$
reducing along the trailing axis of size ``normalized_size``.

returns.description: ``{y, saved_mean, saved_rstd}`` — the activation plus saved stats needed by :meth:`layer_norm_backward`.

notes:
Replaces the 7-op MLX chain (mean → subtract → square → mean →
add+rsqrt → multiply → multiply+add) with one fused kernel.
Mac Studio Llama-scale forward: 2.4 ms → ~0.6 ms.

methods.name: layer_norm_backward
methods.signature: int layer_norm_backward(const int & x, const int & gamma, const int & saved_mean, const int & saved_rstd, const int & grad, int outer, int normalized_size, const int & x_shape, const int & gamma_shape, const int & beta_shape, Dtype dt)
methods.summary: LayerNorm backward.
methods.extended:
Dispatches to :func:`gpu::mps::layer_norm_backward` for large
``normalized_size`` (see :func:`should_dispatch_layer_norm_backward`);
otherwise composes the gradient as a single MLX expression
using the saved mean / rstd.

returns.description: ``{dx, dgamma, dbeta}``.

methods.name: batch_norm_forward
methods.signature: int batch_norm_forward(const int & x, const int & gamma, const int & beta, int, int channels, int, int ndim, double eps, const int & x_shape_param, Dtype dt)
methods.summary: BatchNorm forward (training mode).
methods.extended:
**Math**

Per-channel reduction over batch + spatial dims:
$\mu_c = \mathbb{E}[x_{n,c,\ldots}]$,
$\mathrm{rstd}_c = (\operatorname{Var}[x_{n,c,\ldots}] + \varepsilon)^{-1/2}$,
$y = \gamma (x - \mu) \mathrm{rstd} + \beta$.

returns.description: ``{y, saved_mean, saved_rstd}``.

notes:
For very large activations (ImageNet-scale) dispatches to
:func:`gpu::mps::batch_norm_train_forward` per
:func:`should_dispatch_batch_norm_train`; otherwise hand-rolled
MLX reductions over channel axes.

methods.name: batch_norm_backward
methods.signature: int batch_norm_backward(const int & x, const int & gamma, const int & saved_mean, const int & saved_rstd, const int & saved_xnorm, const int & grad, int, int channels, int, int ndim, const int & x_shape_param, Dtype dt, double eps)
methods.summary: BatchNorm backward (training mode).

returns.description: ``{dx, dgamma, dbeta}``.  Dispatches to MPSGraph on large activations; falls back to a MLX composite for ResNet-scale shapes where MLX already matches reference parity.

methods.summary: Matrix / vector norm.  Routed to MLX's CPU linalg stream per H3.
methods.extended:
**Math**

Generic ``ord``-norm along ``axes``.  For ``ord=2`` and a matrix
axis pair this is the Frobenius / spectral norm depending on
mode; for a vector axis it is the L2 norm.
notes:
MLX implements ``linalg::norm`` on the CPU stream, so the result
physically lives in unified memory but the compute happens on
the CPU (H3 carve-out).

methods.name: nn_fold
methods.signature: int nn_fold(const int & x, const int & x_shape, const int & out_shape, const int & kernel_size, const int & stride, const int & padding, const int & dilation, Dtype dt)
methods.summary: fold (col2im) — Metal-native via ``scatter_add_axis``.
methods.extended:
Algorithm: precompute on CPU a length-(kH*kW*L) table of flat output
positions (oh*outW + ow) for every (ki, kj, l_idx) triple plus a
matching validity mask.  Upload both as small MLX arrays.  Then a
single ``scatter_add_axis`` call accumulates the (N, C, kH*kW, L)
input slices into the (N, C, outH*outW) output buffer along axis 2.
Invalid (out-of-bounds) source elements are zeroed via the mask, so
they scatter +0 to position 0 and have no effect on the result.
``nn.Fold`` — reverse of ``nn.Unfold``: scatters unfolded patches back into a feature map.
notes:
Built atop MLX's ``scatter_add`` because patches may overlap and
must accumulate.

(``mean`` / ``sum`` / ``none``).

methods.summary: Huber loss (smooth L1).
methods.extended:
**Math**

else $\delta \, (|x - t| - 0.5 \delta)$.

methods.summary: Binary cross-entropy from probability inputs (not logits).
methods.extended:
**Math**

$L = -(t \log p + (1 - t) \log (1 - p))$ then reduced.

methods.summary: Binary cross-entropy from logits, numerically stable.
methods.extended:
**Math**

$L = \max(x, 0) - x t + \log(1 + e^{-|x|})$ — the standard
log-sum-exp-stable form.

methods.name: conv_nd_forward
methods.signature: int conv_nd_forward(const int & x, const int & W, const int & b, int B, int Cin, int Cout, int Cin_g, int Cout_g, const int * S, const int * K, const int * O, const ConvNdOpts & opts, const int & out_shape, Dtype dt)
methods.extended:
**Math**

$$y_{n,c_o,p_1,\ldots,p_N} = b_{c_o} + \sum_{c_i} \sum_{\Delta} W_{c_o,c_i,\Delta} \, x_{n,c_i,p_1 s_1 + \Delta_1,\ldots}$$
where $s$ is the stride and the sum runs over the kernel window.
notes:
MLX uses NHWC (channels-last) ordering, so this routine permutes
input + weight NCHW → NHWC, dispatches ``mlx::core::conv1d`` /
``conv2d`` / ``conv3d`` based on ``ndim``, then permutes the
result back to NCHW.  See ``gpu_nchw_to_nhwc_perm`` family.

methods.summary: N-D convolution backward producing ``{dx, dW, db}``.

returns.description: ``{dx, dW, db}`` in that order; ``db`` may be a stub when the forward had no bias.

notes:
Like the forward, this routes through the NHWC permutation
helpers.  Heavy lifting is two extra MLX convolutions (one for
each weight slot) plus a reduction for ``db``.

methods.name: mlx_unary
methods.signature: int mlx_unary()
methods.summary: Applies a unary MLX operation fn to the underlying array and wraps the result in a new GpuStorage.
methods.extended:
No mlx::core::contiguous() call here: element-wise MLX ops (exp, sin,
abs, relu, …) always write to a freshly allocated C-contiguous Metal
buffer regardless of the input strides.  Adding contiguous() would insert
a redundant lazy graph node that MLX must inspect and discard at eval
time, adding ~150 µs of overhead per call on 10M-element float32 arrays.

If an op genuinely needs the output to be contiguous (e.g. a view op),
call mlx_unary_contiguous() instead or wrap explicitly.

methods.name: mlx_unary_contiguous
methods.signature: int mlx_unary_contiguous()
methods.summary: Variant of mlx_unary that forces C-contiguous output.  Use only for ops that may produce strided/non-contiguous results (e.g. view ops).

methods.name: mlx_binary
methods.signature: int mlx_binary()
methods.summary: Applies a binary MLX operation fn to two GpuStorage arrays.  Shape is ignored because MLX handles broadcasting internally.
methods.extended:
Same rationale as mlx_unary: binary element-wise ops always produce
C-contiguous output in MLX, so contiguous() is not needed here.

methods.signature: int mlx_reduce()
methods.summary: Applies an MLX reduction fn(array, axes, keepdims).  If opts.axes is empty all axes are reduced (full reduction).
methods.extended: MLX reductions always produce C-contiguous output; contiguous() omitted.

methods.summary: 3.4+ Phase A.1: fused ReLU backward.  Single MLX expression replacing the prior 3-op composition (greater + astype + multiply) which MLX did not fuse — the cast from Bool to F32 between greater and multiply broke the kernel-fusion chain.  ``where(greater(x, 0), g, 0)`` is a single conditional-select kernel that handles the typecast implicitly. members.summary: Concrete GPU backend routing every Lucid op through Apple's MLX library.
members.extended:
Inherits :class:`IBackend` and overrides each per-op virtual to call
the matching ``mlx::core::*`` primitive (broadcast, matmul, reduce,
activations, …).  Results are MLX lazy arrays — ``mx::eval()`` is
invoked when the engine needs the concrete buffer (e.g. for
:class:`MetalKernelRunner` or :func:`download_gpu_to_cpu`).  Stream
tagging uses :class:`Dispatcher`'s default GPU stream unless an op
requests another (linalg ops dispatch to a CPU MLX stream per the
H3 carve-out, since MLX's eigh / svd / qr run on CPU).

**See Also**

:class:`CpuBackend` — Accelerate-backed sibling.
:class:`MlxBridge` — Storage ↔ mlx::core::array conversion.
:struct:`GpuBackendRegistrar` — static-init registration.
examples: Construction is automatic via static registration::
notes:
- CPU and GPU backends are static singletons registered with the
  :class:`Dispatcher` at process start (see :struct:`GpuBackendRegistrar`).
- All Storage values passed in MUST hold ``GpuStorage`` — the
  Dispatcher guarantees this by routing Device::GPU tensors here.
- MLX uses lazy evaluation; operations build a graph but defer
  execution until ``eval()`` or a CPU read forces it.
- ``Dtype::F64`` is unsupported on the GPU (Metal limitation); callers
  must cast to F32 or keep the tensor on the CPU.
- Convolutions internally permute NCHW ↔ NHWC because MLX expects
  channels-last; see ``gpu_nchw_to_nhwc_perm`` family of helpers.

attributes.name: device()
attributes.description: Always returns ``Device::GPU``.

members.name: AxisIndexUpdates
members.signature: AxisIndexUpdates
members.summary: MLX's ``scatter_{max,min,prod}`` lack an axis-only variant the way ``scatter_add`` does (``scatter_add_axis``).  For axis-scatter we reach the multi-axis API but use **K=1** with a single index array reshaped to (1,...,N,...,1) — same trick MLX would do internally for ``scatter_max(a, idx, src, axis)``.
members.extended:
For Lucid's axis-scatter ``a[..., idx[i,j,...], ...] op= src[i,j,...]``
along ``dim``: idx and src have identical shape (≤ a along non-dim
axes).  MLX's multi-axis scatter expects updates of shape
``idx_shape + a.shape[K..]``; with K=1 and reshaping idx so the dim
axis sits at the head, MLX broadcasts updates back across non-dim
axes for us.

methods.name: contiguous
methods.signature: int contiguous(const int & src, const int & shape, const int & stride, int storage_offset, bool already_contiguous, Dtype dt)
methods.summary: Returns a contiguous copy of a (possibly strided or offset) view.
methods.extended:
Walks the stride / offset layout to produce a densely-packed
output buffer suitable for kernels that assume row-major
contiguous data.

parameters.name: src
parameters.description: Source buffer (may overlap a larger allocation).

parameters.name: stride
parameters.description: Per-dimension byte (or element, depending on backend) stride.

parameters.name: storage_offset
parameters.description: Byte offset from the start of ``src`` where the view begins.

parameters.name: already_contiguous
parameters.description: Hint that the layout is already row-major contiguous; enables a fast ``memcpy`` path when combined with ``storage_offset == 0``.

returns.description: Densely-packed copy of the view.

methods.name: add
methods.signature: int add(const int & a, const int & b, const int & shape, Dtype dt)
methods.summary: Elementwise arithmetic on two broadcast-prepared operands.
methods.extended:
Each of the five primitives below computes ``out[i] = a[i] op b[i]``
for every element, with both ``a`` and ``b`` already broadcast (by
the caller) to the common output ``shape``.

**Math**

$$\mathrm{out}[i] = \begin{cases}
  a[i] + b[i] & \text{add} \\
  a[i] - b[i] & \text{sub} \\
  a[i] \cdot b[i] & \text{mul} \\
  a[i] / b[i] & \text{div} \\
  a[i]^{b[i]} & \text{pow}
\end{cases}$$

parameters.name: dt
parameters.description: Element dtype (common to ``a``, ``b``, and the result).

returns.description: Newly-allocated output buffer of the same shape and dtype.

methods.summary: Elementwise unary math ops.
methods.extended:
The group below — :func:`exp`, :func:`log`, :func:`sqrt`,
:func:`rsqrt`, :func:`abs`, :func:`neg`, :func:`sign`,
:func:`floor`, :func:`ceil`, :func:`round`, :func:`sin`,
:func:`cos`, :func:`tanh`, :func:`sigmoid`, :func:`relu` — all
share the same call shape.  Each applies the named scalar
function element-by-element to the flattened buffer; ``shape`` is
used only for ``numel`` computation.

returns.description: Newly-allocated output of the same shape and dtype.

notes:
Dispatch: CPU implementations call ``vForce`` (transcendentals) or
``vDSP`` (algebraic ops); GPU implementations call the matching
``mlx::core::*`` primitive.

methods.signature: int silu(const int & a, const int & shape, Dtype dt)
methods.summary: Activation functions with paired forward / backward kernels.
methods.extended:
Each forward op below has a matching ``*_backward`` that receives
the original pre-activation input ``a`` together with the upstream
gradient ``grad`` and returns the input gradient $\partial
\mathcal{L}/\partial x$.

parameters.name: shape
parameters.description: Common shape of input, output, and gradient.

returns.description: Forward output, or input gradient for the ``*_backward`` form.

notes:
Backward kernels exist as fused single-op delegates so the GPU
backend can route to MPSGraph and the CPU backend to hand-rolled
scalar loops; previously the autograd composed these from
primitive storage ops.

methods.name: silu_backward
methods.signature: int silu_backward(const int & a, const int & grad, const int & shape, Dtype dt)
methods.summary: silu_backward — dL/dx = σ(x) * (1 + x*(1 - σ(x))) * dL/dy.  Lucid's autograd node previously composed this from storage primitives; the single-op delegate lets the GPU backend dispatch to MPSGraph and CPU to a hand-rolled scalar loop.

methods.name: log_softmax
methods.signature: int log_softmax(const int & a, const int & shape, int axis, Dtype dt)
methods.summary: Numerically stable log-softmax along a single axis.
methods.extended:
**Math**

$$y_i = x_i - \log\sum_j \exp(x_j),$$

computed via the max-subtraction trick to avoid overflow.

parameters.name: axis
parameters.description: Dimension to normalise over (may be negative).

returns.description: Log-probabilities of the same shape and dtype as ``a``.

methods.name: log_softmax_backward
methods.signature: int log_softmax_backward(const int & y, const int & grad_out, const int & shape, int axis, Dtype dt)
methods.summary: Backward pass for :func:`log_softmax`.
methods.extended:
**Math**

$$\frac{\partial \mathcal{L}}{\partial x} =
  \frac{\partial \mathcal{L}}{\partial y}
  - \exp(y) \sum_{\text{axis}}\frac{\partial \mathcal{L}}{\partial y},$$

where ``y = log_softmax(x)`` is the saved forward output.

parameters.name: shape
parameters.description: Common shape of ``y``, ``grad_out``, and the returned input gradient.

parameters.name: axis
parameters.description: Axis along which the forward log-softmax was taken.

returns.description: Input gradient $\partial \mathcal{L}/\partial x$.

where ``a`` and ``b`` may have a leading batch dimension and may

notes:
CPU dispatches to ``cblas_sgemm`` / ``cblas_dgemm`` via
Accelerate; GPU dispatches to ``mlx::core::matmul`` (which selects
an MPS or Metal-shader kernel internally).

methods.name: rms_norm_backward
methods.signature: StoragePair rms_norm_backward(const int & x, const int & gamma, const int & saved_rstd, const int & grad, int outer, int normalized_size, const int & x_shape, const int & gamma_shape, Dtype dt)
methods.summary: RMSNorm backward; returns (grad_x, grad_gamma).

methods.signature: int layer_norm_forward(const int & x, const int & gamma, const int & beta, int outer, int normalized_size, double eps, const int & x_shape, Dtype dt)
methods.summary: LayerNorm forward.  Returns [output, saved_mean, saved_rstd].

methods.name: layer_norm_backward
methods.signature: int layer_norm_backward(const int & x, const int & gamma, const int & saved_mean, const int & saved_rstd, const int & grad, int outer, int normalized_size, const int & x_shape, const int & gamma_shape, const int & beta_shape, Dtype dt)
methods.summary: LayerNorm backward; returns [grad_x, grad_gamma, grad_beta].

methods.name: batch_norm_forward
methods.signature: int batch_norm_forward(const int & x, const int & gamma, const int & beta, int batch, int channels, int spatial, int ndim, double eps, const int & x_shape, Dtype dt)
methods.summary: BatchNorm training forward.  ndim distinguishes 1-D, 2-D, and 3-D spatial tensors.  Returns [output, saved_mean, saved_rstd, saved_xnorm].  The 4th element (xnorm = (x - mean) * rstd) is the normalised input that backward needs; the MLX forward path already materialises it as a lazy intermediate, so exposing it costs nothing at forward time and saves a recomputation at backward time.  Backends without a meaningful xnorm intermediate (e.g. the MPSGraph dispatch) may return an empty Storage for slot 3 — the backward will then fall back to recomputing.

methods.name: batch_norm_backward
methods.signature: int batch_norm_backward(const int & x, const int & gamma, const int & saved_mean, const int & saved_rstd, const int & saved_xnorm, const int & grad, int batch, int channels, int spatial, int ndim, const int & x_shape, Dtype dt, double eps)
methods.summary: BatchNorm backward; returns [grad_x, grad_gamma, grad_beta]. `eps` matches the forward's epsilon — GPU backend needs it to reconstruct variance from saved_rstd (var = 1/rstd^2 - eps) for the MPSGraph normalizationGradient* path.  CPU backend may ignore.
methods.extended:
3.4+ Phase A.4: ``saved_xnorm`` optionally carries the forward's
normalised input ``(x - mean) * rstd`` as a full-size tensor (same
shape as ``x``).  When present (i.e. non-empty Storage) the MLX-path
backward consumes it directly and skips two element-wise ops per
BN backward (centered = x - mean; xnorm = centered * rstd).  Passing
an empty Storage triggers the prior recomputation path — required
for the MPSGraph dispatch, which has its own xnorm management.

methods.name: group_norm_forward
methods.signature: int group_norm_forward(const int & x, const int & gamma, const int & beta, int batch, int channels, int spatial, int groups, const int & spatial_dims, double eps, const int & x_shape, Dtype dt)
methods.summary: GroupNorm forward; groups partitions channels into equal groups before normalizing each group independently.  Returns [output, saved_mean, saved_rstd].

methods.name: group_norm_backward
methods.signature: int group_norm_backward(const int & x, const int & gamma, const int & saved_mean, const int & saved_rstd, const int & grad, int batch, int channels, int spatial, int groups, const int & spatial_dims, const int & x_shape, Dtype dt)
methods.summary: GroupNorm backward; returns [grad_x, grad_gamma, grad_beta].

methods.name: linalg_solve
methods.signature: int linalg_solve(const int & a, const int & b, const int & a_shape, const int & b_shape, Dtype dt)
methods.summary: Solves a linear system Ax = b; b may have multiple right-hand sides.

methods.name: linalg_matrix_power
methods.signature: int linalg_matrix_power(const int & a, const int & shape, int power, Dtype dt)
methods.summary: A^power computed via repeated squaring or eigendecomposition.

methods.name: linalg_pinv
methods.signature: int linalg_pinv(const int & a, const int & shape, Dtype dt)
methods.summary: Moore-Penrose pseudoinverse via SVD.

methods.name: linalg_det
methods.signature: int linalg_det(const int & a, const int & shape, Dtype dt)
methods.summary: Determinant (scalar per batch element).

methods.name: linalg_qr
methods.signature: StoragePair linalg_qr(const int & a, const int & shape, const int & q_shape, const int & r_shape, Dtype dt)
methods.summary: Reduced QR decomposition; returns (Q, R).

methods.name: linalg_eig
methods.signature: StoragePair linalg_eig(const int & a, const int & shape, const int & values_shape, const int & vectors_shape, Dtype dt)
methods.summary: General eigendecomposition for non-symmetric matrices; returns (eigenvalues, eigenvectors). Eigenvalues may be complex; CPU backend returns packed real+imag components.

methods.name: linalg_eigh
methods.signature: StoragePair linalg_eigh(const int & a, const int & shape, const int & values_shape, const int & vectors_shape, Dtype dt)
methods.summary: Symmetric/Hermitian eigendecomposition (real eigenvalues guaranteed). Returns (eigenvalues, eigenvectors); eigenvectors are column-ordered.

methods.name: linalg_svd
methods.signature: int linalg_svd(const int & a, const int & shape, bool compute_uv, const int & u_shape, const int & s_shape, const int & vt_shape, Dtype dt)
methods.summary: Singular value decomposition.  compute_uv=false returns only singular values S.  Returns [U, S, Vt] or [S] depending on compute_uv.

methods.name: linalg_lu_factor
methods.signature: StoragePair linalg_lu_factor(const int & a, const int & shape, Dtype dt)
methods.summary: LU factorisation (packed format).  Returns {LU, pivots} where LU is the packed n×n matrix (LAPACK dgetrf_ format) and pivots is an n-element I32 tensor of 1-based pivot indices.

methods.name: linalg_solve_triangular
methods.signature: int linalg_solve_triangular(const int & a, const int & b, const int & a_shape, const int & b_shape, bool upper, bool unitriangular, Dtype dt)
methods.summary: Triangular solve: solve A X = B where A is triangular. upper=true → upper triangular; unit=true → unit diagonal.

methods.name: linalg_lstsq
methods.signature: int linalg_lstsq(const int & a, const int & b, const int & a_shape, const int & b_shape, Dtype dt)
methods.summary: Least-squares: solve min||AX-B||_2. Returns [solution, residuals, rank, svd]. a_shape=(m,n), b_shape=(m,nrhs). Solution shape=(n,nrhs).

methods.name: linalg_lu_solve
methods.signature: int linalg_lu_solve(const int & LU, const int & pivots, const int & b, const int & lu_shape, const int & b_shape, Dtype dt)
methods.summary: Solve AX=B given LU+pivots from linalg_lu_factor. Returns X (same shape as B).

methods.name: linalg_householder_product
methods.signature: int linalg_householder_product(const int & H, const int & tau, const int & h_shape, Dtype dt)
methods.summary: Reconstruct Q (m×k) from Householder reflectors H (m×n) and tau (k,).

methods.name: linalg_ldl_factor
methods.signature: StoragePair linalg_ldl_factor(const int & a, const int & shape, Dtype dt)
methods.summary: LDL^T factorization of symmetric matrix. Returns {LD_packed, pivots_i32}.

methods.name: broadcast
methods.signature: int broadcast(const int & a, const int & src_shape, const int & dst_shape, Dtype dt)
methods.summary: Broadcasts `a` from src_shape to dst_shape following NumPy rules.

methods.name: repeat
methods.signature: int repeat(const int & a, const int & shape, Dtype dt, int repeats, int axis)
methods.summary: Repeats elements `repeats` times along `axis` (equivalent to np.repeat).

methods.name: tile
methods.signature: int tile(const int & a, const int & shape, Dtype dt, const int & reps)
methods.summary: Tiles the tensor by replicating it reps[i] times along each dimension.

methods.name: permute
methods.signature: int permute(const int & a, const int & shape, const int & perm, Dtype dt)
methods.summary: Permutes dimensions according to `perm` (equivalent to np.transpose).

methods.name: pad
methods.signature: int pad(const int & a, const int & shape, Dtype dt, const int)
methods.summary: Zero-pads (or reflects/wraps) the tensor with pad_width[d] = (before, after) extra elements for each dimension d.  constant specifies the fill value.

methods.name: pow_scalar
methods.signature: int pow_scalar(const int & a, const int & shape, Dtype dt, double exp)
methods.summary: Raises each element to a scalar exponent: a**exp.

methods.name: rpow_scalar
methods.signature: int rpow_scalar(const int & a, const int & shape, Dtype dt, double base)
methods.summary: Raises a scalar base to the power of each element: base**a.

methods.name: clip
methods.signature: int clip(const int & a, const int & shape, Dtype dt, double min_v, double max_v)
methods.summary: Clamps each element to [min_v, max_v].

methods.name: cast
methods.signature: int cast(const int & a, const int & shape, Dtype src_dt, Dtype dst_dt)
methods.summary: Type-casts the buffer element-by-element from src_dt to dst_dt.

methods.name: mse_loss
methods.signature: int mse_loss(const int & input, const int & target, const int & shape, Dtype dt, int reduction)
methods.summary: Mean-squared error: 0.5*(input - target)^2 summed/averaged. Backward returns (grad_input, grad_target).

methods.name: mse_loss_backward
methods.signature: int mse_loss_backward(const int & input, const int & target, const int & grad, const int & shape, Dtype dt, int reduction)

methods.name: huber_loss
methods.signature: int huber_loss(const int & input, const int & target, const int & shape, Dtype dt, double delta, int reduction)
methods.summary: Huber loss (smooth L1): quadratic for |r|<=delta, linear otherwise. delta controls the transition point between the two regimes. Backward returns (grad_input, grad_target).

methods.name: huber_loss_backward
methods.signature: int huber_loss_backward(const int & input, const int & target, const int & grad, const int & shape, Dtype dt, double delta, int reduction)

methods.name: bce_loss
methods.signature: int bce_loss(const int & input, const int & target, const int & weight, const int & shape, Dtype dt, double eps, int reduction)

methods.name: bce_loss_backward
methods.signature: int bce_loss_backward(const int & input, const int & target, const int & weight, const int & grad, const int & shape, Dtype dt, double eps, int reduction)

methods.name: bce_with_logits_loss
methods.signature: int bce_with_logits_loss(const int & input, const int & target, const int & weight, const int & pos_weight, const int & shape, const int & weight_shape, const int & pos_weight_shape, Dtype dt, int reduction)

methods.name: bce_with_logits_backward
methods.signature: int bce_with_logits_backward(const int & input, const int & target, const int & weight, const int & pos_weight, const int & grad, const int & shape, Dtype dt, int reduction)

methods.name: cross_entropy_loss
methods.signature: ClassLossForwardResult cross_entropy_loss(const int & input, const int & target, const int * weight, const int & input_shape, const int & target_shape, Dtype dt, double eps, int ignore_index, int reduction)
methods.summary: Cross-entropy loss with optional per-class weights and ignore_index. saved_aux inside the result holds the log-softmax activations needed by the backward pass.  weight is optional (may be nullptr for unweighted CE).

methods.name: cross_entropy_backward
methods.signature: int cross_entropy_backward(const int & saved_softmax, const int & target, const int * weight, const int & valid_count, const int & grad, const int & input_shape, Dtype dt, int ignore_index, int reduction)
methods.summary: Cross-entropy backward; saved_softmax is the softmax output stored during the forward.  valid_count accounts for ignored-index samples in mean reduction.

methods.name: nll_loss
methods.signature: ClassLossForwardResult nll_loss(const int & input, const int & target, const int * weight, const int & input_shape, const int & target_shape, Dtype dt, int ignore_index, int reduction)
methods.summary: Negative log-likelihood loss; operates on log-probabilities directly (unlike cross_entropy which applies log-softmax internally).

methods.name: nll_loss_backward
methods.signature: int nll_loss_backward(const int & target, const int * weight, const int & valid_count, const int & grad, const int & input_shape, Dtype dt, int ignore_index, int reduction)
methods.summary: NLL backward; returns the input gradient.

methods.name: to_cpu
methods.signature: CpuStorage to_cpu(const int & a, const int & shape)
methods.summary: Materialises the tensor's data in CPU-accessible memory.

returns.description: Host-side aligned buffer containing the evaluated data.

notes:
For :class:`CpuBackend` this is effectively a copy of the existing
``CpuStorage``; for :class:`GpuBackend` it calls
``mlx::core::array::eval()`` first (triggering execution of the
pending lazy graph) and then copies the result into a fresh
``CpuStorage`` on the host.

methods.name: sdpa_forward
methods.signature: int sdpa_forward(const int & q, const int & k, const int & v, const int * attn_mask, const int & q_shape, const int & k_shape, const int & v_shape, Dtype mask_dtype, int mask_numel, double scale, bool is_causal, Dtype dt)
methods.summary: Scaled dot-product attention forward.  Returns [output, saved_weights] where saved_weights holds the post-softmax attention scores for backward. is_causal=true applies a lower-triangular mask to prevent attending to future tokens without materialising the full mask tensor.

methods.name: sdpa_backward
methods.signature: int sdpa_backward(const int & grad_out, const int & q, const int & k, const int & v, const int & saved_weights, const int & q_shape, const int & k_shape, const int & v_shape, double scale, Dtype dt)
methods.summary: SDPA backward; returns [grad_q, grad_k, grad_v].

methods.name: conv_transpose_nd_forward
methods.signature: int conv_transpose_nd_forward(const int & x, const int & W, const int & b, int B, int Cin, int Cout, const int * S, const int * K, const int * O, const int * stride, const int * pad, const int * opad, int N, const int & out_shape, Dtype dt)

methods.name: conv_transpose_nd_backward
methods.signature: int conv_transpose_nd_backward(const int & grad_out, const int & x, const int & W, int B, int Cin, int Cout, const int * S, const int * K, const int * O, const int * stride, const int * pad, int N, Dtype dt)

methods.name: conv_nd_forward
methods.signature: int conv_nd_forward(const int & x, const int & W, const int & b, int B, int Cin, int Cout, int Cin_g, int Cout_g, const int * S, const int * K, const int * O, const ConvNdOpts & opts, const int & out_shape, Dtype dt)

methods.name: conv_nd_backward
methods.signature: int conv_nd_backward(const int & grad_out, const int & x, const int & W, int B, int Cin, int Cout, int Cin_g, int Cout_g, const int * S, const int * K, const int * O, const ConvNdOpts & opts, Dtype dt)

methods.name: unfold_forward
methods.signature: int unfold_forward(const int & x, int B, int C, const int & S, const int & K, const int & O, const int & stride, const int & pad, const int & dilation, const int & out_shape, Dtype dt)

methods.name: unfold_backward
methods.signature: int unfold_backward(const int & grad_out, int B, int C, const int & S, const int & K, const int & O, const int & stride, const int & pad, const int & dilation, Dtype dt)

methods.name: expand_and_multiply
methods.signature: int expand_and_multiply(const int & mask, const int & x, const int & mask_shape, const int & x_shape, Dtype dt)

methods.name: drop_block_mask
methods.signature: int drop_block_mask(const int & seed, double drop_prob, int block_size, const int & x_shape, Dtype dt)

methods.name: embedding_forward
methods.signature: int embedding_forward(const int & weight, const int & indices, const int & weight_shape, const int & indices_shape, const int & out_shape, int padding_idx, Dtype dt)
methods.summary: Embedding table lookup: out[i] = weight[indices[i]]. padding_idx entries are zeroed in the output.

methods.name: embedding_backward
methods.signature: int embedding_backward(const int & grad_out, const int & indices, const int & weight_shape, const int & indices_shape, int padding_idx, Dtype dt)
methods.summary: Backward pass for embedding: scatter-adds grad_out rows into a zero-filled weight gradient of weight_shape.

methods.name: nn_fold
methods.signature: int nn_fold(const int & x, const int & x_shape, const int & out_shape, const int & kernel_size, const int & stride, const int & padding, const int & dilation, Dtype dt)
methods.summary: Fold (col2im): inverse of unfold/im2col. Accumulates (N, C*kH*kW, L) patches back into (N, C, outH, outW) using scatter-add.

methods.name: embedding_bag_forward
methods.signature: int embedding_bag_forward(const int & weight, const int & indices, const int & offsets, const int & weight_shape, const int & indices_shape, int mode, int padding_idx, bool include_last_offset, Dtype dt)
methods.summary: EmbeddingBag: gather rows from weight at indices, then reduce per bag. mode: 0=sum, 1=mean, 2=max. For 1-D indices, offsets marks bag starts.

methods.name: flip
methods.signature: int flip(const int & a, const int & shape, const int & dims, Dtype dt)
methods.summary: Flip (reverse) along the given axes.

methods.name: masked_select_count
methods.signature: int masked_select_count(const int & mask, const int & shape, Dtype dt)
methods.summary: Masked select: extract elements where bool mask == true. Returns a flat 1-D Storage of `n_true` elements.

methods.name: masked_select
methods.signature: int masked_select(const int & a, const int & mask, const int & a_shape, const int & mask_shape, int n_true, Dtype dt)

methods.name: ctc_loss_forward
methods.signature: int ctc_loss_forward(const int & log_probs, const int & targets, const int & input_lengths, const int & target_lengths, const int & lp_shape, int blank, bool zero_infinity, Dtype dt)
methods.summary: CTC (Connectionist Temporal Classification) loss. log_probs: (T, N, C) log-probabilities; targets: (N, S) or flat (sum_S,) input_lengths: (N,); target_lengths: (N,). Returns per-sample losses of shape (N,) before reduction.

methods.name: sinusoidal_pos_embedding
methods.signature: int sinusoidal_pos_embedding(int seq_len, int embed_dim, Dtype dt)
methods.summary: Generates a fixed sinusoidal positional encoding of shape (seq_len, embed_dim) following the original Transformer paper formula.

methods.name: rope_forward
methods.signature: int rope_forward(const int & x, const int * position_ids, const int & x_shape, bool interleaved, Dtype pos_dtype, Dtype dt)
methods.summary: Rotary Position Embedding (RoPE) forward.  Returns [output, cos_cache, sin_cache]. interleaved=true uses GPT-NeoX layout; false uses Llama layout. position_ids is optional; if null, positions 0..seq_len-1 are assumed.

methods.name: rope_backward
methods.signature: int rope_backward(const int & grad_out, const int & saved_cos, const int & saved_sin, const int & x_shape, bool interleaved, Dtype dt)
methods.summary: RoPE backward; applies the inverse rotation using cached cos/sin values.

methods.name: max_pool_nd_forward
methods.signature: int max_pool_nd_forward(const int & x, const int & x_shape, const int & out_shape, const PoolOpts & opts, Dtype dt)
methods.summary: N-D max pooling forward.  Returns [output, argmax]; argmax stores the flat index within the spatial window that produced each maximum, required by max_pool_nd_backward to route gradients.

methods.name: max_pool_nd_backward
methods.signature: int max_pool_nd_backward(const int & grad_out, const int & saved_argmax, const int & x_shape, const int & out_shape, const PoolOpts & opts, Dtype dt)
methods.summary: N-D max pooling backward; scatters grad_out to the argmax positions.

methods.name: avg_pool_nd_forward
methods.signature: int avg_pool_nd_forward(const int & x, const int & x_shape, const int & out_shape, const PoolOpts & opts, Dtype dt)
methods.summary: N-D average pooling forward (count-include-pad semantics by default).

methods.name: avg_pool_nd_backward
methods.signature: int avg_pool_nd_backward(const int & grad_out, const int & x_shape, const int & out_shape, const PoolOpts & opts, Dtype dt)
methods.summary: N-D average pooling backward; distributes grad uniformly across each window.

methods.name: batch_norm_eval_forward
methods.signature: int batch_norm_eval_forward(const int & x, const int & mean, const int & var, const int & gamma, const int & beta, const int & x_shape, int C, int spatial, double eps, Dtype dt)
methods.summary: BatchNorm inference-mode forward; uses running statistics (mean, var) rather than computing batch statistics.  Returns [output, rstd].

methods.name: batch_norm_eval_backward
methods.signature: int batch_norm_eval_backward(const int & x, const int & mean, const int & gamma, const int & rstd, const int & grad_out, const int & x_shape, int C, int spatial, Dtype dt)
methods.summary: BatchNorm inference-mode backward; returns [grad_x, grad_gamma, grad_beta].

methods.name: lp_normalize_forward
methods.signature: int lp_normalize_forward(const int & x, const int & x_shape, double ord, int axis, double eps, Dtype dt)
methods.summary: Lp-normalizes `x` along `axis` using the given norm order `ord`. Returns [output, saved_norm] so the backward can reuse the computed norm.

methods.name: lp_normalize_backward
methods.signature: int lp_normalize_backward(const int & x, const int & saved_norm, const int & grad_out, const int & x_shape, double ord, int axis, Dtype dt)
methods.summary: Lp-normalize backward; saved_norm is the per-row norm from the forward.

methods.name: global_response_norm_forward
methods.signature: int global_response_norm_forward(const int & x, const int & gamma, const int & beta, const int & x_shape, double eps, Dtype dt)
methods.summary: Global Response Normalization (GRN) from ConvNeXt-v2. Returns [output, saved_Nx] where Nx is the per-channel L2 norm.

methods.name: global_response_norm_backward
methods.signature: int global_response_norm_backward(const int & x, const int & gamma, const int & beta, const int & saved_Nx, const int & grad_out, const int & x_shape, double eps, Dtype dt)
methods.summary: GRN backward; returns [grad_x, grad_gamma, grad_beta].

methods.name: interpolate_nearest_2d_forward
methods.signature: int interpolate_nearest_2d_forward(const int & input, const int & in_shape, int H_out, int W_out, Dtype dt)
methods.summary: Nearest-neighbor upsampling for 2-D and 3-D feature maps.

methods.name: interpolate_nearest_3d_forward
methods.signature: int interpolate_nearest_3d_forward(const int & input, const int & in_shape, int D_out, int H_out, int W_out, Dtype dt)

methods.name: interpolate_bilinear_forward
methods.signature: int interpolate_bilinear_forward(const int & input, const int & in_shape, int H_out, int W_out, bool align_corners, Dtype dt)
methods.summary: Bilinear interpolation forward/backward. align_corners=true maps corner pixels to corner coordinates; false scales by factor (in_size / out_size).

methods.name: interpolate_bilinear_backward
methods.signature: int interpolate_bilinear_backward(const int & grad_out, const int & in_shape, int H_out, int W_out, bool align_corners, Dtype dt)

methods.name: interpolate_trilinear_forward
methods.signature: int interpolate_trilinear_forward(const int & input, const int & in_shape, int D_out, int H_out, int W_out, bool align_corners, Dtype dt)
methods.summary: Trilinear interpolation for volumetric feature maps.

methods.name: interpolate_trilinear_backward
methods.signature: int interpolate_trilinear_backward(const int & grad_out, const int & in_shape, int D_out, int H_out, int W_out, bool align_corners, Dtype dt)

methods.name: affine_grid_forward
methods.signature: int affine_grid_forward(const int & theta, int N, int H, int W, bool align_corners, Dtype dt)
methods.summary: Affine grid generation for spatial transformer networks. theta is an (N, 2, 3) affine matrix; output has shape (N, H, W, 2).

methods.name: affine_grid_backward
methods.signature: int affine_grid_backward(const int & grad_grid, int N, int H, int W, bool align_corners, Dtype dt)
methods.summary: Affine grid backward: returns grad_theta of shape (N, 2, 3).

methods.name: grid_sample_forward
methods.signature: int grid_sample_forward(const int & input, const int & grid, const int & in_shape, const int & grid_shape, int mode, int padding_mode, bool align_corners, Dtype dt)
methods.summary: Samples input at grid locations using bilinear (mode=0) or nearest (mode=1) interpolation.  padding_mode: 0=zeros, 1=border, 2=reflection.

methods.name: grid_sample_backward
methods.signature: int grid_sample_backward(const int & grad_out, const int & input, const int & grid, const int & in_shape, const int & grid_shape, int mode, int padding_mode, bool align_corners, Dtype dt)
methods.summary: Grid sample backward; returns [grad_input, grad_grid].

methods.name: bilinear_layer_forward
methods.signature: int bilinear_layer_forward(const int & x1, const int & x2, const int & weight, const int & bias, bool has_bias, const int & x1_shape, const int & x2_shape, const int & w_shape, Dtype dt)

methods.name: bilinear_layer_backward
methods.signature: int bilinear_layer_backward(const int & grad_out, const int & x1, const int & x2, const int & weight, const int & x1_shape, const int & x2_shape, const int & w_shape, bool has_bias, Dtype dt)

methods.name: one_hot_forward
methods.signature: int one_hot_forward(const int & indices, const int & indices_shape, int num_classes, Dtype out_dtype)
methods.summary: One-hot encoding: produces a (num_classes,)-wide indicator tensor for each index in `indices`.  Output dtype is typically F32 or I32.

methods.name: rotate_forward
methods.signature: int rotate_forward(const int & input, const int & shape, double angle_rad_neg, double cx, double cy, Dtype dt)
methods.summary: Rotates a 2-D image by angle_rad_neg (stored negated for efficiency) around center (cx, cy) using bilinear interpolation.

methods.name: ge_mask
methods.signature: int ge_mask(const int & a, const int & b, const int & shape, Dtype dt)
methods.summary: Element-wise masks that return 0.0/1.0 float results. ge_mask: out[i] = (a[i] >= b[i]) ? 1 : 0 lt_mask: out[i] = (a[i] < b[i])  ? 1 : 0

methods.name: lt_mask
methods.signature: int lt_mask(const int & a, const int & b, const int & shape, Dtype dt)

methods.name: add_scalar
methods.signature: int add_scalar(const int & a, const int & shape, Dtype dt, double scalar)
methods.summary: Broadcasts a scalar to the array and adds/multiplies element-wise.

methods.name: mul_scalar
methods.signature: int mul_scalar(const int & a, const int & shape, Dtype dt, double scalar)

methods.name: in_range_mask
methods.signature: int in_range_mask(const int & a, const int & shape, Dtype dt, double lo, double hi)
methods.summary: Returns a 0/1 mask where a[i] is in the closed interval [lo, hi].

methods.name: leaky_mask
methods.signature: int leaky_mask(const int & a, const int & shape, Dtype dt, double slope)
methods.summary: Returns slope*a[i] when a[i]<0, else a[i].  Used inside leaky-relu backward.

methods.name: positive_mask
methods.signature: int positive_mask(const int & a, const int & shape, Dtype dt)
methods.summary: Returns 1.0 where a[i] > 0, else 0.0.  Used in relu backward.

methods.name: relu_backward
methods.signature: int relu_backward(const int & g, const int & x, const int & shape, Dtype dt)
methods.summary: Fused ReLU backward: returns ``g`` where ``x > 0``, else ``0``.  Single- kernel alternative to ``multiply(g, positive_mask(x))`` — collapses the 3-op chain (greater + astype + multiply) into one MLX expression that MLX can fuse natively (or that GPU dispatches as ``where``).  Falls back to the 2-step composition on backends without a fused path.

methods.name: reduce_grad_to_shape
methods.signature: int reduce_grad_to_shape(const int & grad, const int & grad_shape, const int & target_shape, Dtype dt)
methods.summary: Reduces `grad` (of shape grad_shape) to target_shape by summing over the broadcast dimensions.  Used in broadcast backward passes.

methods.name: broadcast_back_for_reduce
methods.signature: int broadcast_back_for_reduce(const int & grad, const int & grad_shape, const int & input_shape, const int & axes, bool keepdims, Dtype dt)
methods.summary: Broadcasts `grad` back to input_shape after a reduction.  The inverse of reduce_sum when keepdims=false.

methods.name: full
methods.signature: int full(const int & shape, Dtype dt, double fill_value)
methods.summary: Allocates a tensor filled with fill_value.

methods.name: eye
methods.signature: int eye(int N, int M, int k, Dtype dt)
methods.summary: Constructs an M×N identity-like matrix with the diagonal at offset k.

methods.name: diag
methods.signature: int diag(const int & v, const int & v_shape, int k, Dtype dt, int & out_shape)
methods.summary: Extracts a 1-D diagonal at offset k from a 2-D matrix, or places a 1-D vector on a 2-D diagonal.  out_shape is written with the result shape.

methods.name: tri
methods.signature: int tri(const int & input, const int & shape, Dtype dt, int k, bool upper)
methods.summary: Applies a triangular mask: upper=true zeroes the lower triangle, upper=false zeroes the upper triangle, relative to diagonal offset k.

methods.name: floordiv
methods.signature: int floordiv(const int & a, const int & b, const int & shape, Dtype dt)
methods.summary: Integer floor division: out[i] = floor(a[i] / b[i]).

methods.name: inner
methods.signature: int inner(const int & a, const int & b, const int & a_shape, const int & b_shape, const int & out_shape, Dtype dt)
methods.summary: Inner product generalised to batches: contracts the last axis of `a` against the last axis of `b`.

methods.name: permute_cpu
methods.signature: CpuStorage permute_cpu(const CpuStorage & src, const int & src_shape, const int & perm, Dtype dt)
methods.summary: Permutes a CpuStorage buffer in-place and returns the result as a new CpuStorage.  Used for data layout conversions before GPU upload.

methods.name: nonzero_forward
methods.signature: CpuStorage nonzero_forward(const int & input, const int & input_shape, Dtype input_dtype, int & numel_out)
methods.summary: Returns a CpuStorage containing the flat indices of all nonzero elements. numel_out is set to the number of nonzero elements found.

methods.name: fused_linear_relu_forward
methods.signature: int fused_linear_relu_forward(const int & x, const int & w, const int & b, const int & out_shape, Dtype dt)
methods.summary: Optional fused kernels; default implementations call the base backend's linear() followed by relu/gelu and throw if the dtype is unsupported. Backends that can fuse these two ops (e.g. ANE) override them.

methods.name: fused_linear_gelu_forward
methods.signature: int fused_linear_gelu_forward(const int & x, const int & w, const int & b, const int & out_shape, Dtype dt)

methods.name: run_custom_metal_kernel
methods.signature: int run_custom_metal_kernel(const int & kernel_source, const int & function_name, const int & inputs, const int & output_shape, Dtype output_dtype, const int & grid, const int & threads)
methods.summary: Compiles and launches a user-provided Metal Shading Language (MSL) kernel. Default implementation throws not_implemented; only GpuBackend overrides this. grid and threads follow Metal's threadgroups / threadsPerThreadgroup convention.

methods.signature: int astype(const int & a, const int & shape, Dtype src_dt, Dtype dst_dt)
methods.summary: Cast elements to a different dtype; shape is unchanged. CPU: element-wise static_cast loop.  GPU: mlx::core::astype.

methods.signature: int to_shared_storage(const int & src, const int &)
methods.summary: Moves `src` to MTLResourceStorageModeShared memory so that both CPU and GPU can access it without a copy.  Default is a no-op; GpuBackend overrides.

methods.name: lstm_forward
methods.signature: int lstm_forward(const int & input, const int & h0, const int & c0, const int & weights, const LstmOpts & opts, const int & out_shape, Dtype dt)
methods.summary: LSTM inference forward.  Returns [output, hn, cn]. Default implementation throws not_implemented; concrete backends override.
