methods.signature: int lstm_forward_train(const int & input, const int & h0, const int & c0, const int & weights, const LstmOpts & opts, Dtype dt)
methods.summary: LSTM training forward; saves gate and cell activations for BPTT. Returns [output, hn, cn, gates_all, cells_all].

methods.name: lstm_backward
methods.signature: int lstm_backward(const int & grad_output, const int & grad_hn, const int & grad_cn, const int & input, const int & h0, const int & weights, const int & gates_all, const int & cells_all, const LstmOpts & opts, Dtype dt)
methods.summary: LSTM BPTT backward.  gates_all and cells_all are the saved activations from lstm_forward_train.  Returns [dX, dh0, dc0, dW_ih, dW_hh, db_ih, db_hh].

members.summary: Abstract compute backend interface.
members.extended:
One concrete :class:`IBackend` exists per :class:`Device` slot (CPU,
GPU); :class:`Dispatcher` holds them and routes every op based on the
tensor's device tag.  Implementations dispatch the actual math to
their device-native library — Apple Accelerate (vDSP / vForce / BLAS
/ LAPACK) for CPU, MLX for GPU.

**See Also**

:class:`CpuBackend` — Accelerate-backed concrete CPU.
:class:`GpuBackend` — MLX-backed concrete GPU.
:class:`Dispatcher` — selects which to use.
notes:
**Ownership.**  Each concrete backend is created once and stored
inside the :class:`Dispatcher` singleton via ``std::unique_ptr``; the
lifetime of every :class:`IBackend` therefore equals the lifetime of
the process.

**Thread safety.**  Methods are *thread-compatible* but not internally
synchronised: the engine never calls into the same backend from
multiple threads simultaneously on the same op, though distinct ops
on distinct tensors may overlap.  Callers that share a backend across
threads must provide their own serialisation, or rely on the
underlying framework (e.g. MLX's own thread-safety guarantees).

**Design rationale.**  All methods take :class:`Storage` by
const-reference and return a new :class:`Storage`; no in-place
mutation is exposed at this layer.  Concrete implementations choose
the most efficient internal representation — ``CpuStorage`` (Apple
aligned host buffer) for CPU, ``GpuStorage`` (``mlx::core::array``)
for GPU.

members.name: ConvNdOpts
members.signature: ConvNdOpts
members.summary: Compact parameter bag for N-D standard convolution.
members.extended:
Used by :func:`IBackend::conv_nd_forward` and
:func:`conv_nd_backward` for ``N`` in ``{1, 2, 3}``.  The
fixed-size arrays are indexed ``0..N-1``; any unused trailing
entries are ignored by the implementation.

attributes.name: N
attributes.description: Number of spatial dimensions (1, 2, or 3).

attributes.name: groups
attributes.description: Channel grouping factor; ``1`` means standard convolution and ``Cin`` means depthwise.

attributes.name: stride, pad, dilation
attributes.description: Per-spatial-dimension geometry (only the first ``N`` entries are meaningful).

members.name: LstmOpts
members.signature: LstmOpts
members.summary: Parameter bag for LSTM operations.
members.extended:
Used by :func:`lstm_forward`, :func:`lstm_forward_train`, and
:func:`lstm_backward`.  ``weights`` is supplied separately as
``std::vector<Storage>`` in the order
``[W_ih, W_hh, b_ih, b_hh]`` per layer; bidirectional layers
double the count (forward block followed by reverse block).

When ``proj_size > 0`` (the projected-LSTM / LSTMP variant) an
additional weight tensor ``W_hr`` of shape ``(proj_size,
hidden_size)`` is appended per layer so the order becomes
``[W_ih, W_hh, b_ih, b_hh, W_hr]``.  The recurrent weight
``W_hh`` then has its second axis sized ``proj_size`` instead of
``hidden_size`` because the projected hidden state feeds the
next time step.  The cell state ``c_n`` keeps shape
``hidden_size`` regardless.

attributes.name: input_size, hidden_size
attributes.description: Input feature size and recurrent hidden size.

attributes.name: num_layers
attributes.description: Number of stacked LSTM layers.

attributes.name: seq_len, batch_size
attributes.description: Time and batch dimensions of the input sequence.

attributes.name: batch_first
attributes.description: If ``true`` the input is laid out as ``(batch, seq, input)``; otherwise ``(seq, batch, input)``.

attributes.name: bidirectional
attributes.description: Whether to run a reverse-direction stack alongside the forward.

attributes.name: has_bias
attributes.description: Whether the per-gate bias terms are present.

attributes.name: proj_size
attributes.description: ``0`` ⇒ standard LSTM; ``> 0`` ⇒ projected (LSTMP) variant.

members.name: PoolOpts
members.signature: PoolOpts
members.summary: Compact parameter bag for N-D pooling.
members.extended:
Used by :func:`max_pool_nd_forward`, :func:`avg_pool_nd_forward`,
and their backward counterparts for ``N`` in ``{1, 2, 3}``.

attributes.name: K
attributes.description: Kernel size per spatial dimension.

attributes.name: stride
attributes.description: Stride per spatial dimension.

attributes.name: pad
attributes.description: Symmetric (both-sides) padding per spatial dimension.

attributes.name: N
attributes.description: Number of spatial dimensions actually used.

members.name: MatmulOpts
members.signature: MatmulOpts
members.summary: Parameter bag for a single matrix-multiplication call.
members.extended:
Carries the logical shape and transposition flags for one ``a @ b``
product.  Dimensions are interpreted in *logical* (not transposed)
order so the result is always $(M \times K) \cdot (K \times N) = (M
\times N)$ regardless of the ``transA`` / ``transB`` flags.

**See Also**

:func:`IBackend::matmul` — consumes this struct.

attributes.name: transA
attributes.description: If ``true`` the dispatched kernel treats the left operand as transposed before contraction.

attributes.name: transB
attributes.description: If ``true`` the dispatched kernel treats the right operand as transposed before contraction.

attributes.name: M
attributes.description: Number of rows of the (post-transpose) left operand.

attributes.name: K
attributes.description: Contracted inner dimension.

attributes.name: N
attributes.description: Number of columns of the (post-transpose) right operand.

attributes.name: batch
attributes.description: Number of independent matmuls stacked along the leading axis. ``batch == 1`` is the standard 2-D case; ``batch > 1`` indicates a batched matmul where the same A/B strides repeat ``batch`` times.

members.name: ReduceOpts
members.signature: ReduceOpts
members.summary: Parameter bag for axis-reduction operations.
members.extended:
Used by :func:`IBackend::reduce_sum`, :func:`reduce_mean`,
:func:`variance`, :func:`reduce_max`, :func:`reduce_min`, and similar
axis-collapsing ops.

**See Also**

:func:`IBackend::reduce_sum` — primary consumer.

attributes.name: axes
attributes.description: List of dimensions to reduce over.  Entries may be negative (Python-style) and are normalised by the callee.  An empty list is interpreted as *reduce over all axes*.

attributes.name: keepdims
attributes.description: If ``true`` the collapsed dimensions are retained as size-1 in the output shape; otherwise they are removed.

members.name: StoragePair
members.signature: StoragePair
members.summary: Convenience wrapper bundling two Storage outputs from one op call.
members.extended:
Used by ops that naturally return a pair of arrays — QR
decomposition, eigendecomposition, RMSNorm forward (output + saved
rstd), sort+argsort, LU factor, LDL factor, and similar.

attributes.name: first, second
attributes.description: The two output tensors; the semantic role of each is documented on the method that returns this struct.

members.name: BuilderContext
members.signature: BuilderContext

methods.name: BuilderContext
methods.signature: void BuilderContext(void * graph_void, Device device)
methods.summary: Construct with the active :class:`MPSGraph` (passed as ``__bridge void*``) and the target device.

methods.name: resolve
methods.signature: void * resolve(int id)
methods.summary: Resolve a :type:`TensorId` to its :class:`MPSGraphTensor*`. Returns ``nullptr`` for unregistered ids — used by the builder to detect "input not produced by any traced op" (i.e. external feed candidates).

methods.name: bind
methods.signature: void bind(int id, void * tensor)
methods.summary: Register a :class:`MPSGraphTensor` produced by an emitter (or a placeholder for an external feed) under a :type:`TensorId`.

methods.name: graph
methods.signature: void * graph()
methods.summary: Direct access to the underlying graph + device.  Used inside emitter ``.mm`` files for placeholder creation and op assembly.

methods.name: device
methods.signature: Device device()
methods.summary: Device the executable is being compiled for (e.g. ``Device::GPU``). Emitters route MPSGraph vs Metal-shader paths off this value.

members.summary: Builder-side state threaded through each emitter call.
members.extended:
Holds the active :class:`MPSGraph` plus the id → tensor map that
resolves an input :type:`TensorId` to the :class:`MPSGraphTensor`
produced by an earlier op (or by a placeholder for external feeds).

attributes.name: graph_
attributes.description: The graph being assembled.  Cast back to ``MPSGraph*`` in .mm files via ``__bridge MPSGraph*``.

attributes.name: device_
attributes.description: Device for the trace.  Phase 1.2 supports only ``Device::GPU`` since MPSGraph is Metal-only; CPU traces escape to eager.

attributes.name: tensors_
attributes.description: Resolves a TraceIR id to the :class:`MPSGraphTensor` that represents it inside the graph.

members.name: CacheKey
members.signature: CacheKey

methods.name: operator==
methods.signature: bool operator==(const CacheKey & other)
methods.summary: Structural equality — every field above must match element-wise. Used as the unordered_map bucket-equality predicate.

members.summary: Cache key — the structural signature of a :class:`TraceGraph`.
members.extended:
Two traces share an executable iff every field below matches.  The
input *values* are not part of the key; only the per-op output meta
influences the compiled graph (because MPSGraph placeholders are
shape-static in Phase 1.2 and Phase 1.6 will introduce a separate
``dynamic`` key variant).

attributes.name: op_names
attributes.description: Op-name sequence in dispatch order.  Compared element-wise.

attributes.name: output_shapes
attributes.description: Per-op single-output shape.  Phase 1.2 supports one output per node; Phase 1.3+ may append entries for multi-output ops.

attributes.name: output_dtypes
attributes.description: Per-op single-output dtype.

attributes.name: device
attributes.description: Device on which the executable was compiled (all ops in a trace must agree on one device — Phase 1.2 builder enforces this).

members.name: CacheKeyHash
members.signature: CacheKeyHash

methods.name: operator()
methods.signature: int operator()(const CacheKey & key)
methods.summary: Hash combine over op_names + output_shapes + output_dtypes + device.  Two structurally-equal keys produce the same hash.

members.summary: std-style hasher for :class:`CacheKey`; used as the bucket function for :class:`ExecutableCache`'s internal map.

members.name: ExecutableCache
members.signature: ExecutableCache

methods.name: ExecutableCache
methods.signature: void ExecutableCache()
methods.summary: Construct an empty cache with the default LRU cap (32 entries). Use :func:`set_max_entries` to override before inserting.

methods.name: ~ExecutableCache
methods.signature: void ~ExecutableCache()
methods.summary: Destructor — releases every cached :class:`CompiledExecutable` via :func:`destroy_executable` before the map is torn down.

methods.name: operator=
methods.signature: ExecutableCache & operator=(const ExecutableCache &)

methods.name: find
methods.signature: CompiledExecutable * find(const CacheKey & key)
methods.summary: Look up an executable.
methods.extended:
Returns the borrowed pointer on hit (and bumps the entry to
most-recent in the LRU order) or ``nullptr`` on miss.

methods.name: insert
methods.signature: void insert(CacheKey key, CompiledExecutable * exec)
methods.summary: Insert ``exec`` under ``key``, taking ownership.  Replaces any existing entry (releasing the previous executable).  If the resulting size exceeds ``max_entries``, evicts least-recently used entries until back at capacity.

methods.name: clear
methods.signature: void clear()
methods.summary: Release every stored executable.

methods.name: size
methods.signature: int size()
methods.summary: Number of currently-cached entries.

methods.name: set_max_entries
methods.signature: void set_max_entries(int n)
methods.summary: Override the LRU cap.  Honoured by subsequent inserts; existing entries are not evicted retroactively.  Default is 32.

methods.name: session
methods.signature: ExecutableCache & session()
methods.summary: Process-global session cache.  First call honours the ``LUCID_COMPILE_MAX_CACHE`` environment variable (positive integer).  Subsequent calls return the same instance.

members.summary: Bounded-LRU cache of compiled MPSGraph executables.
members.extended:
Thread-safe.  Stores raw :class:`CompiledExecutable` pointers and
destroys them via :func:`destroy_executable` on eviction or
destruction.

members.name: OpEmitter
members.signature: OpEmitter

methods.name: ~OpEmitter
methods.signature: void ~OpEmitter()
methods.summary: Virtual destructor — ensures derived per-op emitter destructors run when held by the registry's base-class pointer.  Defaulted.

methods.name: emit
methods.signature: void * emit(BuilderContext & ctx, const OpNode & node)
methods.summary: Emit the MPSGraph subgraph for ``node``.

parameters.name: ctx
parameters.description: Carries the in-progress graph and the id → tensor map. Implementations call :func:`BuilderContext::resolve` to find input tensors and :func:`BuilderContext::bind` to register their output(s).

parameters.name: node
parameters.description: The TraceIR node to emit.  Implementations may inspect ``node.inputs`` (TraceIds; a value of ``-1`` indicates an external feed that the builder must materialise as a placeholder before calling this emit) and ``node.outputs`` (shape / dtype metadata for the produced tensor).

returns.description: The op's output tensor inside the graph, or ``nullptr`` to signal "unsupported under this configuration" — the builder aborts and marks the trace signature as eager-only.  The returned tensor is also bound into ``ctx`` automatically by :class:`MpsBuilder` so the next op can consume it.

methods.name: op_name
methods.signature: int op_name()
methods.summary: Op name handled by this emitter — matched against ``OpNode::name``.

members.summary: Abstract emitter — one per op family.
members.extended:
Concrete subclasses live in this directory (``Linear.mm``,
``Conv.mm``, …).  Each subclass registers itself at process startup
via :func:`register_emitter`.

members.name: OpNode
members.signature: OpNode
members.summary: One recorded op in the trace.
notes:
Aggregate — no methods, no invariants enforced at construction.
The Tracer is responsible for maintaining well-formedness (no
dangling input ids, output ids in monotone increasing order).

attributes.name: name
attributes.description: Op name as passed to :class:`OpScopeFull` (e.g. ``"conv2d"``, ``"layer_norm"``).  Matches the schema name in :class:`OpRegistry`.

attributes.name: inputs
attributes.description: Ids of input tensors, in op-defined order.  Phase 1.1 records these by appending to the vector after the backend dispatch returns (the Tracer hook in :class:`OpScopeFull` runs at op entry; input wiring happens later in :func:`wire_autograd`). An :class:`OpNode` with a pending input slot indicates an op that did not go through ``wire_autograd`` — the builder treats that as an eager-only signature.

attributes.name: outputs
attributes.description: Per-output metadata snapshots.  Phase 1.1 has at most one output per node; the vector is generic so multi-output ops (e.g. ``topk``, ``lstm``) can be added without IR changes.

attributes.name: attrs
attributes.description: Optional payload keyed by attribute name.  Populated by op forwards via :func:`OpScopeFull::set_attr` when the emitter needs context that can't be recovered from input + output shapes alone (permutation, axes, stride, padding, eps, …).

members.name: TensorMeta
members.signature: TensorMeta
members.summary: Metadata snapshot of a tensor at the moment it appears in the trace.
notes:
Aggregate — copyable, comparable by member-wise equality.  No
inheritance and no virtual members so the value can be stored in a
:class:`std::vector` with no indirection.

attributes.name: id
attributes.description: Stable id assigned by the Tracer.  Used by :class:`OpNode` to reference inputs and outputs without owning the underlying :class:`TensorImpl`.

attributes.name: shape
attributes.description: Output shape at trace time.  Phase 1.2 emits MPSGraph placeholders with this shape; Phase 1.6 substitutes batch dim with ``-1`` for ``dynamic=True``.

attributes.name: dtype
attributes.description: Element dtype.  Drives F16 vs F32 placeholder choice when the builder consults :func:`current_amp_policy`.

attributes.name: device
attributes.description: Device of the original tensor.  The builder rejects traces containing mixed-device ops; a single :class:`TraceGraph` runs on one device.

members.name: TraceGraph
members.signature: TraceGraph
members.summary: Ordered list of :class:`OpNode` records produced by one trace.
notes:
Aggregate.  Phase 1.2 attaches an :class:`MpsBuilder` cache entry
keyed by ``hash(ops)`` rather than mutating this struct.

attributes.name: ops
attributes.description: Nodes appended in dispatch order.  The list defines a DAG via tensor ids — node ``j`` depends on node ``i`` iff one of ``ops[j].inputs`` equals one of the ids in ``ops[i].outputs``.

attributes.name: next_id
attributes.description: Monotonic counter the Tracer uses to mint fresh ids when wiring outputs.  Owned by :class:`TraceGraph` so concurrent traces on different threads have independent id spaces.

members.name: Tracer
members.signature: Tracer

methods.name: Tracer
methods.signature: void Tracer()
methods.summary: Construct an empty ``Tracer`` ready to be installed via :func:`set_current_tracer`.
methods.extended:
Defaulted; ``graph_`` starts empty and ``impl_to_id_`` starts with
no entries.  No op hooks fire until the tracer is installed as the
active TLS sink — construction itself has zero side effects.

methods.name: operator=
methods.signature: Tracer & operator=(const Tracer &)

methods.name: on_op_enter
methods.signature: void on_op_enter(int name, Device device, Dtype dtype, int shape)
methods.summary: Records the entry of a new op into the active scope.

parameters.name: name
parameters.description: Op name as passed to :class:`OpScopeFull`.  Copied into the resulting :class:`OpNode::name`.

parameters.name: device
parameters.description: Device on which the op is dispatching.  Stored in the single :class:`TensorMeta` appended to ``outputs``.

parameters.name: dtype
parameters.description: Output element dtype at op entry.

parameters.name: shape
parameters.description: Output shape at op entry.  Moved into the appended :class:`TensorMeta`.

notes:
Phase 1.1 appends one :class:`OpNode` per call with an empty
``inputs`` vector and a single-element ``outputs`` vector.
Phase 1.2 backfills ``inputs`` from the autograd wiring site
and may append additional outputs for multi-output ops.

methods.name: on_op_io
methods.signature: void on_op_io(const int & inputs, const int & output)
methods.summary: Wires the input/output TensorImpl identities of the most recently recorded :class:`OpNode` into the trace IR.

parameters.name: inputs
parameters.description: Owning shared pointers to the input tensors in op-defined order.  ``nullptr`` slots (e.g. an optional bias) are passed through as ``-1`` in the resulting :class:`OpNode::inputs``. The Tracer retains a strong reference to every external feed (input not produced by an earlier traced op) so the :class:`MpsBuilder` can read its shape/dtype + the run path can bind its data buffer.

parameters.name: output
parameters.description: Output tensor.  The id minted earlier by :func:`on_op_enter` (stored in ``node.outputs[0].id``) is associated with this pointer in the internal map so subsequent ops that consume this tensor can resolve it back to the same id.  Only the raw pointer is kept (no strong ref) — the output's lifetime is the caller's responsibility.

notes:
Phase 1.1 wires only kernel families that go through
:class:`kernel::NaryKernel` or :class:`kernel::VariadicKernel`'s
``wire_autograd`` boundary (Conv / Linear / BatchNorm / Norm /
Loss / …).  Ops dispatched through :class:`UnaryKernel` or
:class:`BinaryKernel`'s in-line ``forward()`` reach
:func:`on_op_enter` but skip this step — their ``inputs`` slot
stays empty, marking the node "pending" for the Phase 1.2
builder, which then routes that signature to the eager path.

Calling this with an empty :class:`TraceGraph` (no preceding
:func:`on_op_enter`) is a no-op — defensive against ordering
mistakes at hook sites.

methods.name: on_op_attr
methods.signature: void on_op_attr(int key, int value)
methods.summary: Attach a single attribute to the most recently recorded :class:`OpNode`.  Used by op forwards via :func:`OpScopeFull::set_attr` to thread payloads (permutation, axis, stride, padding, …) that the emitter would otherwise be unable to recover from inputs + output shapes alone.

parameters.name: key
parameters.description: Attribute name; chosen by the op forward and read by the matching emitter.  Convention: snake_case, matches the reference-framework keyword argument when one exists (``"permutation"``, ``"stride"``, ``"padding"``, ``"axis"``, ``"keepdim"``, ``"eps"``, …).

parameters.name: value
parameters.description: The payload, moved into the attribute map.

notes:
Calling this with an empty :class:`TraceGraph` (no preceding
:func:`on_op_enter`) is a no-op — defensive against ordering
mistakes at hook sites.  Overwrites any prior value under
``key`` on the same node, so duplicate calls report the last
value (matches ``unordered_map`` semantics).

methods.name: graph
methods.signature: const TraceGraph & graph()
methods.summary: Returns a read-only reference to the recorded graph.

returns.description: The op DAG accumulated so far.  Caller must not access this reference after the Tracer is destroyed or a subsequent :func:`on_op_enter` invalidates iterators into ``ops``.

methods.name: external_feeds
methods.signature: const int & external_feeds()
methods.summary: Returns the id → external :type:`TensorImplPtr` map populated by :func:`on_op_io` when an input wasn't produced by any earlier traced op.  The builder uses this to materialise an MPSGraph placeholder per external feed and to bind the corresponding input data at execution time.
methods.extended:
The Tracer holds an owning :type:`TensorImplPtr` for every
external feed so the underlying buffers cannot be released
mid-trace.

members.summary: Thread-local op recorder consumed by :class:`OpScopeFull`.
members.extended:
Constructing a Tracer is cheap — the underlying :class:`TraceGraph`
starts empty.  Hooks fire only between :func:`set_current_tracer`
(this) and :func:`set_current_tracer` (nullptr); outside that
window the tracer accumulates nothing.
examples: Native usage (Python binding mirrors this)::
notes:
Non-copyable, non-movable — the active-tracer slot is a raw pointer
owned by the caller; copying or moving the Tracer would either
invalidate that pointer or duplicate the recording sink.

attributes.name: graph_
attributes.description: The recorded op DAG.  Appended to by :func:`on_op_enter` and exposed read-only via :func:`graph`.

members.name: KernelLaunchConfig
members.signature: KernelLaunchConfig
members.summary: Threadgroup geometry for :func:`run_metal_kernel`.
members.extended:
Mirrors the two ``MTLSize`` arguments passed to
``dispatchThreadgroups:threadsPerThreadgroup:`` — the outer grid
of threadgroups and the per-threadgroup thread count, both in 3-D.
notes:
Total threads launched = product(grid) * product(threads).  Callers
are responsible for sizing this to cover ``output_shape``.

attributes.name: grid
attributes.description: Threadgroup grid extent (x, y, z).  Defaults to ``{1, 1, 1}``.

attributes.name: threads
attributes.description: Threads per threadgroup (x, y, z).  Defaults to ``{1, 1, 1}``. Total ``threads`` product must not exceed the device's ``maxTotalThreadsPerThreadgroup``.

members.name: MetalBuffer
members.signature: MetalBuffer
members.summary: Plain descriptor for a Metal buffer that backs Lucid's `SharedStorage`.
members.extended:
Holds the raw CPU pointer + opaque MTL handle + size for a Metal
shared-memory buffer.  This struct does not own anything — lifetime is
managed externally (see :struct:`OwnedMetalBuffer` for the RAII form).
On Apple Silicon, ``cpu_ptr`` and ``mtl_handle`` reference the **same
physical pages**, so CPU writes are immediately visible to GPU kernels
(and vice versa) without an explicit transfer.

**See Also**

:struct:`OwnedMetalBuffer` — RAII wrapper around this descriptor.
notes:
Use :func:`make_metal_shared` for everyday allocation — manual
pairing of :func:`allocate_shared` and :func:`deallocate_shared` is
only needed for low-level pool code.

attributes.name: cpu_ptr
attributes.description: CPU-accessible virtual address of the buffer.  ``nullptr`` if the buffer is uninitialised or has been deallocated.

attributes.name: mtl_handle
attributes.description: Opaque ``CFRetain``'d ``id<MTLBuffer>`` cast to ``void*`` to keep this header free of Objective-C types.  ``nullptr`` when invalid.

attributes.name: nbytes
attributes.description: Allocation size in bytes.  Always ``0`` on an uninitialised value.

members.name: MetalKernel
members.signature: MetalKernel

methods.name: MetalKernel
methods.signature: void MetalKernel()
methods.summary: Default-construct an invalid kernel handle.
methods.extended:
Equivalent to a moved-from state — :meth:`is_valid` returns
``false`` until populated by :func:`compile_metal_kernel`.

methods.name: operator=
methods.signature: MetalKernel & operator=(const MetalKernel &)

methods.name: ~MetalKernel
methods.signature: void ~MetalKernel()
methods.summary: Release the pipeline + queue on destruction.

methods.name: is_valid
methods.signature: bool is_valid()
methods.summary: Return ``true`` iff compilation produced a usable pipeline.
methods.extended:
A ``false`` return means either Metal is unavailable on this
system or :func:`compile_metal_kernel` failed silently (e.g.
device offline).  Hard MSL syntax errors throw rather than
returning here.

returns.description: ``true`` iff ``pipeline_state != nullptr``.

methods.name: release_
methods.signature: void release_()
methods.summary: Release the retained Metal objects and clear the handle fields.
notes: Idempotent — safe to call on an already-released kernel.

members.summary: RAII handle for a compiled Metal compute pipeline.
members.extended:
Wraps a ``MTLComputePipelineState`` plus its dedicated
``MTLCommandQueue`` (both ``CFRetain``-ed) so that one pipeline can
be invoked many times without re-compiling MSL source.  The Obj-C
types are erased to ``void*`` here to keep this header includable
from plain C++.  Non-copyable, move-only.

**See Also**

:func:`compile_metal_kernel` — factory that produces these.
:func:`run_metal_kernel` — invokes the compiled pipeline.
notes:
Released in :meth:`release_`.  Move constructors null out the
source so the destructor only releases once.

attributes.name: pipeline_state
attributes.description: Opaque ``id<MTLComputePipelineState>``.  ``nullptr`` if compilation failed.

attributes.name: command_queue
attributes.description: Opaque ``id<MTLCommandQueue>``.  Owned independently from any other queue so kernel launches do not contend with MLX's own queue.

attributes.name: name
attributes.description: The MSL function name compiled into ``pipeline_state``.  Useful for debug logs.

warns.annotation: Callers must not share a single kernel across threads
concurrently — clone the handle or guard with a mutex.

members.name: OwnedMetalBuffer
members.signature: OwnedMetalBuffer
members.summary: RAII-owned variant of :struct:`MetalBuffer`.
members.extended:
Bundles a freshly allocated :struct:`MetalBuffer` with a
``std::shared_ptr`` whose custom deleter calls
:func:`deallocate_shared` when the last reference drops.  Used by
``SharedStorage`` to express ref-counted shared GPU/CPU memory.

**See Also**

:func:`make_metal_shared` — the factory that produces these.

attributes.name: buf
attributes.description: The underlying descriptor (still queryable for ``cpu_ptr`` / ``mtl_handle`` / ``nbytes``).

attributes.name: owner
attributes.description: Ref-counted owner that releases ``buf`` on last drop.  Type- erased to ``void`` because the deleter, not the type, carries the cleanup logic.

Returned by :func:`array_to_buffer` after the array has been
evaluated + its producing kernel has reached the ``available``
status.  All fields refer to memory owned by MLX — callers must
not release ``mtl_buffer`` or outlive the source array.

``BufferView`` referring to it is in flight.

members.name: IKernel
members.signature: IKernel

methods.name: ~IKernel
methods.signature: void ~IKernel()
methods.summary: Virtual destructor — ensures derived ``UnaryKernel`` / ``BinaryKernel`` / ``ReduceKernel`` destructors run when held by a base-class pointer.  Defaulted; nothing to clean up at this layer.

methods.name: name
methods.signature: int name()
methods.summary: Return the canonical short name of this op (e.g. ``"add"``, ``"relu"``).

returns.description: A stable, lifetime-bound view of the op's schema name.  Used by error messages, profiler scopes and schema lookups.

notes:
The returned view aliases ``Derived::schema_v1.name`` in CRTP
kernels, so it is valid for the lifetime of the program.

methods.name: compute
methods.signature: int compute(const int &)
methods.summary: Polymorphic forward entry point.

parameters.name: inputs
parameters.description: Flat ordered list of input storages.  The interpretation (arity, shapes, dtype) is determined by the concrete kernel.

returns.description: The single output storage.

raises.description: The default implementation always throws — concrete kernels are normally invoked via their typed static ``forward()`` (which performs full dtype/shape/device handling).  Override this method only when a kernel must participate in a fully generic dispatch loop.

methods.name: apply
methods.signature: int apply(int grad_out)
methods.summary: Backward entry point — invoked by the autograd engine.

parameters.name: grad_out
parameters.description: The gradient of the loss with respect to this op's output.

returns.description: One gradient :class:`Storage` per forward input, in the same order as the inputs were consumed by ``forward()``.

notes:
CRTP bases override this method to delegate to
``Derived::grad_formula(grad_out)`` and apply broadcast-reduction
back to the original input shapes; concrete ops only implement
the math.

members.summary: Abstract base interface every Lucid op kernel implements.
members.extended:
:class:`IKernel` exposes a minimal polymorphic surface so the
autograd engine, profiler, and testing harnesses can manipulate
kernels without knowing the concrete CRTP type.  Concrete kernels
inherit :class:`IKernel` indirectly via one of the CRTP helpers
(:class:`UnaryKernel`, :class:`BinaryKernel`, :class:`NaryKernel`,
:class:`VariadicKernel`); the CRTP layer supplies correct overrides
of every virtual below.

**See Also**

:class:`UnaryKernel`, :class:`BinaryKernel`, :class:`NaryKernel`,
:class:`VariadicKernel` — CRTP helpers that provide the typed
implementations of :meth:`apply` and (when applicable) :meth:`compute`.
:class:`KernelPolicy` — documentation of the compile-time flags
concrete kernels expose to the CRTP layer.
notes:
The interface is intentionally split: :meth:`compute` is the generic
polymorphic forward entry point, while :meth:`apply` is the backward
entry point called by the autograd engine during reverse-mode
differentiation.  In practice nearly every op routes its forward
through its CRTP base's typed static ``forward()`` (which handles
dtype promotion, contiguity, dispatch and graph wiring) and uses
:meth:`compute` only when invoked through a fully generic harness.

Thread safety: instances are **not** thread-safe.  The framework
constructs a fresh kernel per forward invocation and never shares
it across threads.

members.name: KernelPolicy
members.signature: KernelPolicy
members.summary: Documentation-only enumeration of the compile-time policy flags a concrete kernel may override on its CRTP base.
members.extended:
Derived kernels do not instantiate this struct — they override the
equivalent ``static constexpr bool kSavesInput`` / ``kSavesOutput`` /
``kHasGradient`` members on :class:`UnaryKernel` (or sibling bases).
:class:`KernelPolicy` exists purely so the flag set is documented
in one place and discoverable via codegen tools.

**See Also**

:class:`IKernel` — the abstract base these flags refine.
:class:`UnaryKernel`, :class:`BinaryKernel` — CRTP bases that read
these flags via ``Derived::kSavesInput`` etc.

attributes.name: saves_inputs
attributes.description: Whether ``forward()`` snapshots the input storages into ``saved_inputs_`` for later use in ``apply()`` / ``grad_formula``. Set ``false`` for ops whose backward formula does not need the forward inputs (e.g. ``Add`` whose backward is the identity).

attributes.name: saves_output
attributes.description: Whether ``forward()`` additionally snapshots the output storage into ``saved_output_``.  Useful for ops whose backward is expressed more cheaply in terms of the output (e.g. ``ReLU``, ``Softmax``, ``Pow``).

attributes.name: has_gradient
attributes.description: Whether the op participates in autograd at all.  Setting ``false`` skips graph wiring entirely — used by in-place / non-differentiable ops such as integer cast or copy.

attributes.name: deterministic
attributes.description: Whether the op produces bit-identical outputs for bit-identical inputs.  Currently informational only; consumed by future determinism-mode validators.

members.name: NaryKernel
members.signature: NaryKernel

methods.name: name
methods.signature: int name()
methods.summary: Return the canonical schema name of the concrete op.

methods.name: wire_autograd
methods.signature: bool wire_autograd(int bwd, const int & inputs, const int & out, bool save_ins)
methods.summary: Attach an existing backward node to the autograd graph.

parameters.name: bwd
parameters.description: The backward node instance to install on ``out``.

parameters.name: inputs
parameters.description: The forward inputs in canonical order.  Slots may be ``nullptr`` (e.g. an optional bias).

parameters.name: save_ins
parameters.description: If ``true``, snapshot each non-null input's storage into ``saved_inputs_[i]`` for use during backward.

returns.description: ``true`` when the autograd graph was wired and ``out`` had its ``grad_fn`` installed; ``false`` when grad mode is off or no input requires a gradient (no-op).

notes:
Side effects on success: populates ``bwd``'s dtype/device/output
shape from the first non-null input, fills ``input_shapes_``,
``input_tensors_``, ``saved_inputs_``, ``saved_impl_inputs_``,
records one :class:`Edge` per input via :func:`detail::ensure_grad_fn`,
captures per-input versions for in-place mutation detection, and
marks ``out`` as a non-leaf, requires_grad tensor.

members.summary: CRTP base for fixed-arity ``N``-input op kernels.
members.extended:
Inherits :class:`AutogradNode\<Derived, N\>` so input metadata
(``input_shapes_``, ``saved_inputs_``, ``input_tensors_``,
``saved_impl_inputs_``) is stored in compile-time-sized
:type:`std::array` containers.  Also inherits :class:`IKernel` for
polymorphic invocation.

Unlike :class:`UnaryKernel` and :class:`BinaryKernel`, this base does
**not** provide a ``forward()`` trampoline — N-ary ops have too much
op-specific validation to share a single template.  Instead, derived
ops own their ``forward()`` and call :meth:`wire_autograd` once the
output tensor exists to attach the backward node.

Template Parameters
-------------------
Derived : class
    The concrete CRTP self-type.
N : std::size_t
    The fixed input arity.  Typically 3 or more (use
    :class:`BinaryKernel` for ``N == 2``).

**See Also**

:class:`UnaryKernel`, :class:`BinaryKernel`, :class:`VariadicKernel`.
:class:`IKernel` — the abstract base above the CRTP layer.
notes:
Slot count is exactly ``N``.  Derived ops implement :meth:`apply`
(and optionally :meth:`apply_for_graph`) themselves; no
``grad_formula`` contract is enforced because N-ary backward shapes
vary too widely.

members.name: VariadicKernel
members.signature: VariadicKernel

methods.name: name
methods.signature: int name()
methods.summary: Return the canonical schema name of the concrete op.

methods.name: validate_versions
methods.signature: void validate_versions()
methods.summary: Verify no saved input was mutated in-place between forward and backward.
notes:
Iterates over the runtime input vector and calls
:func:`check_version_match` on each, raising a clear error
(with the op name and slot index) if any version drifted.
Invoked by the autograd engine before :meth:`apply`.

methods.name: wire_autograd
methods.signature: bool wire_autograd(int bwd, const int & inputs, const int & out, bool save_ins)
methods.summary: Attach an existing backward node to the autograd graph.

parameters.name: bwd
parameters.description: The backward node instance to install on ``out``.

parameters.name: inputs
parameters.description: The forward inputs in canonical order; slots may be ``nullptr``.

parameters.name: save_ins
parameters.description: Whether to snapshot each non-null input's storage into ``saved_inputs_v_`` for use during backward.

returns.description: ``true`` when the autograd graph was wired; ``false`` when grad mode is off or no input requires a gradient (no-op).

notes:
Side effects on success: derives dtype/device from the first
non-null input, records ``out_shape_``, populates the per-input
vectors, records one :class:`Edge` per input via
:func:`detail::ensure_grad_fn`, captures versions, and marks
``out`` as a non-leaf requires_grad tensor.

members.summary: CRTP base for runtime-variable-arity op kernels.
members.extended:
Inherits :class:`Node` directly (not :class:`AutogradNode\<Derived, N\>`)
so the saved-input and edge containers can grow at runtime, and
:class:`IKernel` for polymorphic invocation.  Derived ops own their
own ``forward()`` and ``apply()`` — only the autograd wiring helper
is shared.

Template Parameters
-------------------
Derived : class
    The concrete CRTP self-type.

**See Also**

:class:`NaryKernel` — fixed-arity sibling using :type:`std::array`.
:class:`UnaryKernel`, :class:`BinaryKernel`.
:class:`IKernel` — the abstract base above the CRTP layer.
notes:
All inputs must share dtype and device — :meth:`wire_autograd` does
**not** validate this; the concrete op's ``forward()`` is expected
to have already enforced consistency before calling wire_autograd.
:meth:`validate_versions` walks the runtime vector and invokes
:func:`check_version_match` per input.

attributes.name: input_tensors_v_
attributes.description: Weak references to the original inputs, used for in-place mutation version checks.

attributes.name: input_shapes_v_
attributes.description: Shapes of the original inputs, preserved for backward shape math.

attributes.name: out_shape_
attributes.description: Shape of the produced output tensor.

attributes.name: dtype_
attributes.description: Effective dtype, taken from the first non-null input.

attributes.name: device_
attributes.description: Effective device, taken from the first non-null input.

attributes.name: saved_inputs_v_
attributes.description: Snapshots of each input's :class:`Storage` at forward time, populated when :meth:`wire_autograd` is called with ``save_ins=true``.

attributes.name: saved_output_
attributes.description: Optional snapshot of the output storage for ops whose backward needs the forward output value (e.g. softmax-style ops).

members.name: NdMatmulInfo
members.signature: NdMatmulInfo
members.summary: Precomputed plan for an N-dimensional batched matrix multiplication.
members.extended:
Resolves both operand shapes into a single triplet of broadcast batch
dimensions plus the inner $(M, K, N)$ contraction tuple, so that the
downstream GEMM loop can iterate a flat batch counter without
re-inspecting per-axis shape information.

**See Also**

plan_nd_matmul : Builds an ``NdMatmulInfo`` from two operand shapes.
cpu_matmul_nd  : Consumes an ``NdMatmulInfo`` and runs the GEMM loop.

attributes.name: out_shape
attributes.description: Shape of the matmul output: broadcast batch dims concatenated with ``[M, N]``.

attributes.name: a_bcast_shape
attributes.description: Shape that operand $A$ is conceptually broadcast to before the GEMM loop — batch dims concatenated with ``[M, K]``.

attributes.name: b_bcast_shape
attributes.description: Shape that operand $B$ is conceptually broadcast to — batch dims concatenated with ``[K, N]``.

attributes.name: M
attributes.description: Row count of each per-batch left matrix.

attributes.name: K
attributes.description: Shared inner (contraction) dimension.

attributes.name: N
attributes.description: Column count of each per-batch right matrix.

attributes.name: batch
attributes.description: Product of all broadcast batch dimensions; the number of 2-D GEMM calls dispatched in the inner loop.

members.name: Err
members.signature: ErrorPayload Err(ErrorCode code, int msg)
members.summary: Builds an :cpp:struct:`ErrorPayload` from a code and message.
members.extended:
The returned payload is implicitly convertible to ``Result<T>`` for any
``T``, so a ``return Err(code, msg);`` inside a function returning
``Result<Something>`` Just Works.

returns.description: Error descriptor ready to be returned as the failure arm of a ``Result``.

members.name: Ok
members.signature: Result<T> Ok()
members.summary: Builds a successful :cpp:class:`Result` from the given value.

parameters.name: value
parameters.description: Payload to wrap.  Moved into the success arm.

returns.description: A result for which :cpp:func:`Result::is_ok` is ``true``.

members.name: T_op
members.signature: int T_op(const int & a)
members.summary: Backing function for the ``.T`` tensor property — alias of ``transpose_op``.
members.extended:
**See Also**

transpose_op : Identical functionality; this alias exists for the
    Python-side ``.T`` property binding.

returns.description: View of ``a`` with reversed axis order.

members.name: abi_version
members.signature: int abi_version()
members.summary: Returns the engine's ABI generation counter at runtime.

returns.description: Value of :macro:`LUCID_ABI_VERSION` at the time the engine shared library was built.

examples: Pseudo-code for the import-time check::

if (engine.abi_version() != LUCID_ABI_VERSION)
        throw ImportError("Lucid ABI mismatch …");
notes:
The Python bindings call this on first import and compare it
against the constant baked into the extension module's translation
unit.  A mismatch indicates that the loaded ``.dylib`` was built
from headers different from the ones the bindings saw, and the
import is aborted with a diagnostic naming both versions.

members.name: abs_inplace_op
members.signature: int abs_inplace_op(const int & a)
members.summary: In-place absolute value: writes $|x|$ back into ``a``.
members.extended:
**Math**

$$a_i \leftarrow |a_i|$$

**See Also**

:func:`abs_op` — out-of-place counterpart.

parameters.name: a
parameters.description: Tensor to take absolute value of in place.  Must be non-null.

returns.description: The same pointer as ``a``.

members.name: abs_op
members.signature: int abs_op(const int & a)
members.summary: Compute $y = |x|$ element-wise.
members.extended:
Allocates a fresh output of the same shape and dtype as ``a`` and
delegates to :class:`AbsBackward::forward`.

**See Also**

:class:`AbsBackward` — backward node.

returns.description: Output tensor of the same shape and dtype, with non-negative values.

members.name: abs_storage
members.signature: int abs_storage(const int & s, int numel, Dtype dt, Device device)
members.summary: Element-wise absolute value: returns :math:`|s|`.

returns.description: New storage holding ``abs(s)``.

members.name: accumulate_into
members.signature: void accumulate_into(int & dst, const int & src)
members.summary: Accumulate ``src`` into ``dst`` element-wise (``dst += src``).
members.extended:
Handles all three :class:`Storage` variants:

- ``CpuStorage / CpuStorage`` — hand-written typed loop for
  ``F32 / F64 / I32 / I64``.
- ``GpuStorage / GpuStorage`` — MLX add followed by rewrap.
- ``SharedStorage`` variants — obtain a ``CpuStorage`` view of the
  shared region and delegate to the CPU path.

raises.description: ``dst`` and ``src`` have different element dtypes.
``dst`` and ``src`` belong to different device categories.

notes:
The most common caller is :class:`AccumulateGrad`, which uses this to
fold each newly-arrived gradient into a leaf's running ``.grad``.

members.name: acos_storage
members.signature: int acos_storage(const int & s, int numel, Dtype dt, Device device)
members.summary: Element-wise arccosine: returns :math:`\arccos(s)`.

returns.description: New storage holding ``acos(s)`` in radians.

members.name: adaptive_avg_pool1d_op
members.signature: int adaptive_avg_pool1d_op(const int & x, int OL)
members.summary: One-dimensional adaptive average-pool to a fixed output length.
members.extended:
Reduces the length axis of a 3-D input to exactly ``OL`` positions by
taking the arithmetic mean over each contiguous segment.  Setting
``OL = 1`` produces *global average pooling* along the sequence.

parameters.name: OL
parameters.description: Target output length.  Must satisfy ``L % OL == 0``.

returns.description: Output of shape ``(B, C, OL)``.

raises.description: If ``x`` is not 3-D.
If ``L`` is not evenly divisible by ``OL``.

members.name: adaptive_avg_pool2d_op
members.signature: int adaptive_avg_pool2d_op(const int & x, int OH, int OW)
members.summary: Two-dimensional adaptive average-pool to a fixed ``(OH, OW)`` grid.
members.extended:
The canonical global average pooling head of modern classification
backbones (ResNet, EfficientNet, ConvNeXt, ...) uses
``OH = OW = 1``.

parameters.name: x
parameters.description: Input of shape ``(B, C, H, W)``.

parameters.name: OH, OW
parameters.description: Target output extents.  Must satisfy ``H % OH == 0`` and ``W % OW == 0``.

returns.description: Output of shape ``(B, C, OH, OW)``.

raises.description: If ``x`` is not 4-D.
If any spatial axis is not evenly divisible by its target.

members.name: adaptive_avg_pool3d_op
members.signature: int adaptive_avg_pool3d_op(const int & x, int OD, int OH, int OW)
members.summary: Three-dimensional adaptive average-pool to a fixed ``(OD, OH, OW)`` volume.
members.extended:
Used as the final pooling layer in 3-D classification networks
(3D-ResNet, SlowFast).  ``OD = OH = OW = 1`` yields the volumetric
global-average-pool head.

parameters.name: x
parameters.description: Input of shape ``(B, C, D, H, W)``.

parameters.name: OD, OH, OW
parameters.description: Target output extents.  Each must evenly divide the corresponding input axis.

returns.description: Output of shape ``(B, C, OD, OH, OW)``.

raises.description: If ``x`` is not 5-D.
If any spatial axis is not evenly divisible by its target.

members.name: adaptive_max_pool1d_op
members.signature: int adaptive_max_pool1d_op(const int & x, int OL)
members.summary: One-dimensional adaptive max-pool to a fixed output length.
members.extended:
Reduces the length axis of a 3-D input to exactly ``OL`` positions by
taking the maximum over each contiguous segment.  When ``OL == 1``
this is *global* max pooling along the sequence.

parameters.name: OL
parameters.description: Target output length.  Must satisfy ``L % OL == 0``.

returns.description: Output of shape ``(B, C, OL)``.

raises.description: If ``x`` is not 3-D.
If ``L`` is not evenly divisible by ``OL`` (non-uniform partitioning is not yet supported).

members.name: adaptive_max_pool2d_op
members.signature: int adaptive_max_pool2d_op(const int & x, int OH, int OW)
members.summary: Two-dimensional adaptive max-pool to a fixed ``(OH, OW)`` grid.
members.extended:
Used in detection / segmentation models that must produce a fixed
spatial grid regardless of input resolution (e.g. RoIAlign-style
heads, classification networks fine-tuned on multi-scale data).

parameters.name: x
parameters.description: Input of shape ``(B, C, H, W)``.

parameters.name: OH, OW
parameters.description: Target output extents.  Must satisfy ``H % OH == 0`` and ``W % OW == 0``.

returns.description: Output of shape ``(B, C, OH, OW)``.

raises.description: If ``x`` is not 4-D.
If any spatial axis is not evenly divisible by its target.

members.name: adaptive_max_pool3d_op
members.signature: int adaptive_max_pool3d_op(const int & x, int OD, int OH, int OW)
members.summary: Three-dimensional adaptive max-pool to a fixed ``(OD, OH, OW)`` volume.
members.extended:
Adaptive variant of ``max_pool3d_op`` for video classification and
volumetric backbones that must accept clips of arbitrary duration.

parameters.name: x
parameters.description: Input of shape ``(B, C, D, H, W)``.

parameters.name: OD, OH, OW
parameters.description: Target output extents.  All three of ``D``, ``H``, ``W`` must be evenly divisible by the corresponding target.

returns.description: Output of shape ``(B, C, OD, OH, OW)``.

raises.description: If ``x`` is not 5-D.
If any spatial axis is not evenly divisible by its target.

members.name: add_inplace_op
members.signature: int add_inplace_op(const int & a, const int & b)
members.summary: In-place elementwise addition: writes $a + b$ back into ``a``.
members.extended:
Equivalent to ``a = add_op(a, b)`` but reuses ``a``'s storage and
bumps its version counter.  ``b`` may broadcast up to ``a``'s shape
but the broadcast output shape must equal ``a->shape()``.

**Math**

$$a_i \leftarrow a_i + b_i$$

**See Also**

:func:`add_op` — out-of-place counterpart.

parameters.name: a
parameters.description: Left operand and in-place target.  Must be non-null and must not share storage with a view.

parameters.name: b
parameters.description: Right operand.  Must be non-null and have a shape broadcastable to ``a->shape()`` such that the broadcast result equals ``a->shape()``.

returns.description: The same pointer as ``a``, now holding the summed storage.

raises.description: If the broadcast output of ``add_op(a, b)`` does not equal ``a->shape()`` (i.e. ``a`` would have to grow to fit the result).
If ``a`` shares storage with a view (call ``.clone()`` first).

members.name: add_op
members.signature: int add_op(const int & a, const int & b)
members.summary: Public entry point — element-wise addition with autograd support.
members.extended:
**Math**

$$ y = a + b $$

**See Also**

sub_op, mul_op, div_op

parameters.name: a, b
parameters.description: Operands of any rank.  Shapes must be NumPy-broadcast-compatible.

raises.description: If ``a`` and ``b`` have incompatible shapes under NumPy broadcasting rules, or if their devices differ.

examples: >>> auto c = add_op(a, b);    // c = a + b, same as a + b in Python

members.name: all_op
members.signature: int all_op(const int & a)
members.summary: Full-tensor boolean AND-reduction — returns a scalar Bool tensor that is ``True`` iff every element of ``a`` is non-zero.
members.extended:
Not differentiable: predicate output is detached.

**Math**

$$y = \bigwedge_i (x_i \ne 0)$$

**Shape**

Output is a 0-D scalar; input may have any shape.

**See Also**

:func:`any_op`.

parameters.name: a
parameters.description: Input tensor of any dtype.  Floating-point ``0.0`` counts as ``False``; ``NaN`` counts as ``True``.

returns.description: Zero-dimensional ``Dtype::Bool`` tensor.  Shape is ``{}``.

notes:
Reduction is over the *flattened* tensor.  An empty tensor returns
``True`` (vacuous truth) — call sites should special-case zero numel
if a different convention is needed.

members.name: allocate_aligned_bytes
members.signature: int allocate_aligned_bytes(int nbytes, Device device)
members.summary: Allocates an aligned, optionally-pooled byte buffer of ``nbytes``.
members.extended:
The returned ``shared_ptr<std::byte[]>`` owns the block via a custom
deleter that either (a) returns the block to the thread-local pool —
for CPU allocations up to 4 MB whose class still has free slots — or
(b) calls ``std::free`` directly, for large allocations, full pool
slots, and non-CPU devices.  In both paths the deleter also fires the
matching :func:`MemoryTracker::track_free` hook.

On every successful allocation the matching
:func:`MemoryTracker::track_alloc` hook is fired, so per-device
counters stay in sync with the live byte total.

parameters.name: nbytes
parameters.description: Number of bytes to allocate.  A value of ``0`` returns an empty ``shared_ptr`` (no allocation, no tracker update).

parameters.name: device
parameters.description: Device tag controlling (a) which :class:`MemoryTracker` counter bank is updated and (b) whether the pool path is taken — only ``Device::CPU`` is poolable.  Defaults to :attr:`Device::CPU`.

returns.description: Owning handle to a ``kCpuAlignment``-aligned byte buffer at least ``nbytes`` long.  Empty when ``nbytes == 0``.  The capacity may exceed ``nbytes`` for pooled allocations (rounded up to the next power of two ≥ ``kCpuAlignment``).

raises.description: ``posix_memalign`` failed.  The exception carries the requested size, the current live bytes, and the historical peak for the target device so the caller can format an informative message.

examples:
auto block = allocate_aligned_bytes(1024);                // CPU, pooled
    auto huge  = allocate_aligned_bytes(64 << 20);            // CPU, bypass pool
    auto gpu   = allocate_aligned_bytes(N, Device::GPU);      // GPU, tracker only
notes:
The pool is **thread-local**, so allocation and deallocation are
lock-free on the fast path.  Cached blocks are released when the
owning thread exits.  Blocks freed on a different thread than the
one that allocated them still work correctly, but they will be
returned to the freeing thread's pool — long-running thread-pool
workloads with cross-thread tensor ownership may therefore observe
slight imbalance in retained free-list size.

members.signature: int alpha_dropout_op(const int & a, double p, bool training, Generator * gen)

members.name: active_dtype
members.signature: int active_dtype()
members.summary: Returns the active autocast dtype on the calling thread.
members.extended:
Reads the thread-local AMP state established by the innermost live
:class:`AutocastGuard`.  Returns ``std::nullopt`` when no guard is
active — callers can use this to skip the AMP dispatch path entirely.

returns.description: The autocast target dtype if AMP is active on this thread, or ``std::nullopt`` otherwise.

notes:
All AMP state is ``thread_local``; different Python threads (or OpenMP
workers) can each maintain independent autocast contexts without
synchronisation.

members.name: is_active
members.signature: bool is_active()
members.summary: Returns true when AMP is active on the calling thread.
members.extended:
Equivalent to ``active_dtype().has_value()`` but avoids constructing
an ``std::optional`` on the hot dispatch path.

returns.description: ``true`` if at least one :class:`AutocastGuard` is currently live on this thread, ``false`` otherwise.

members.name: astype_op
members.signature: int astype_op(const int & a, Dtype dst_dtype)
members.summary: Cast ``a`` to ``dst_dtype``, preserving shape and (when grad mode is enabled) wiring an ``AstypeBackward`` node that casts the gradient back during backward.

parameters.name: dst_dtype
parameters.description: Target dtype for the output.  Floating <-> integer casts are value-preserving (clamp / round semantics live in the backend's ``astype`` kernel, not in a raw reinterpret).

returns.description: New tensor with dtype ``dst_dtype`` and the same shape as ``a``. If ``a.dtype() == dst_dtype`` the function returns ``a`` itself so the existing ``grad_fn`` chain is preserved — earlier versions allocated a fresh ``TensorImpl`` here which silently broke autograd routing under AMP.

notes:
Used internally by the AMP-aware kernels (Linear / Conv / Matmul /
BatchNorm) to cast inputs to the effective compute dtype while keeping
the backward chain intact.  Argmin / argmax reductions also call this
to materialise their integer outputs; those callers do not enable
gradients, so the autograd wiring path is skipped and the cost stays
identical to a raw backend ``astype``.

members.name: atan2_op
members.signature: int atan2_op(const int & y, const int & x)
members.summary: Quadrant-aware arctangent: $y = \mathrm{atan2}(y_\text{in}, x_\text{in})$.
members.extended:
Composite over :func:`arctan_op` plus :func:`where_op` branches that
patch the result with $\pm \pi$ / $\pm \pi/2$ / $0$ corrections based on
the signs of the operands.  The divide $y / x$ is guarded by
substituting $1$ for $x = 0$ so the safe branch is always defined.
Gradient flows through the underlying primitives.

**Math**

$$
  \mathrm{atan2}(y, x) =
  \begin{cases}
    \arctan(y/x) & x > 0 \\
    \arctan(y/x) + \pi & x < 0,\ y \ge 0 \\
    \arctan(y/x) - \pi & x < 0,\ y < 0 \\
    +\pi/2 & x = 0,\ y > 0 \\
    -\pi/2 & x = 0,\ y < 0 \\
    0 & x = 0,\ y = 0
  \end{cases}
$$

**See Also**

:func:`arctan_op`.

returns.description: Angles in $[-\pi, \pi]$ on the broadcast shape of ``y`` and ``x``.

members.name: atan_storage
members.signature: int atan_storage(const int & s, int numel, Dtype dt, Device device)
members.summary: Element-wise arctangent: returns :math:`\arctan(s)`.

returns.description: New storage holding ``atan(s)`` in radians.

members.name: avg_pool1d_forward_f32
members.signature: void avg_pool1d_forward_f32(const float * x, float * y, int B, int C, int L, int KL, int OL, int stride_l, int pad_l)
members.summary: Single-precision 1-D average-pool forward with count-include-pad semantics.
members.extended:
Every output cell is the mean of its ``KL``-wide window with the divisor
fixed at ``KL`` regardless of how many positions fall on padding (pad
values contribute ``0`` to the sum but still count toward the divisor).
This matches the count-include-pad convention used by the reference
framework's default ``AvgPool1d``.

**Math**

$$y_{b,c,o} = \frac{1}{\text{KL}} \sum_{k=0}^{\text{KL}-1}
  x_{b,c,\,o\cdot \text{stride\_l} + k - \text{pad\_l}}.$$

parameters.name: x
parameters.description: Input tensor of shape ``(B, C, L)``.

parameters.name: y
parameters.description: Output tensor of shape ``(B, C, OL)``.

parameters.description: Input tensor of shape ``(B, C, L)``.

parameters.name: y

parameters.name: B, C, L, KL, OL, stride_l, pad_l

members.name: avg_pool2d_forward_f32
members.signature: void avg_pool2d_forward_f32(const float * x, float * y, int B, int C, int H, int W, int KH, int KW, int OH, int OW, int stride_h, int stride_w, int pad_h, int pad_w)

members.extended:
Every output cell is the mean of its ``KH × KW`` window with divisor fixed
at ``KH * KW`` regardless of how many positions fall on padding; padded
positions contribute ``0`` to the sum but still count toward the divisor.

**Math**

$$y_{b,c,oh,ow} = \frac{1}{\text{KH}\,\text{KW}} \sum_{kh, kw} x_{b, c, ih, iw},$$
with $ih = oh\cdot\text{stride\_h} + kh - \text{pad\_h}$, similarly for $iw$.

parameters.description: Window stride and symmetric zero-pad on each axis.

members.name: avg_pool3d_forward_f32
members.signature: void avg_pool3d_forward_f32(const float * x, float * y, int B, int C, int D, int H, int W, int KD, int KH, int KW, int OD, int OH, int OW, int sd, int sh, int sw, int pd, int ph, int pw)
members.summary: Single-precision 3-D average-pool forward with count-include-pad semantics.
members.extended:
Each output voxel is the mean of its ``KD × KH × KW`` window with divisor
fixed at ``KD * KH * KW``; padded positions contribute ``0`` to the sum
but still count toward the divisor.

**Math**

$$y_{b,c,od,oh,ow} = \frac{1}{\text{KD}\,\text{KH}\,\text{KW}}
  \sum_{kd,kh,kw} x_{b,c, id, ih, iw},$$
with $id = od\cdot\text{sd} + kd - \text{pd}$, similarly for $ih, iw$.

parameters.name: x
parameters.description: Input tensor of shape ``(B, C, D, H, W)``.

parameters.name: y
parameters.description: Output tensor of shape ``(B, C, OD, OH, OW)``.

members.name: col2im_1d_f32
members.signature: void col2im_1d_f32(const float * cols, float * dx, int C, int L, int KL, int OL, int stride_l, int pad_l, int dilation_l)
members.summary: Single-precision 1-D col2im fold (gradient transform).
members.extended:
Inverse of ``im2col_1d_f32``: scatter-adds each column of ``cols`` back
into the corresponding positions of $dx$.  Overlapping receptive fields
(stride < kernel) accumulate by sum — this is the mathematically correct
adjoint that the conv1d backward needs.

**Math**

$$ dx[c, l] = \sum_{o, k : o \cdot s + k \cdot d - p = l} \text{cols}[c \cdot KL + k, o] $$

parameters.name: dx
parameters.description: Output gradient tensor, shape $(C, L)$; **must be zeroed by the caller** before this call.

parameters.name: C, L, KL, OL
parameters.description: Channel count, input length, kernel length, output length.

members.name: dgemm
members.signature: void dgemm(bool transA, bool transB, int M, int N, int K, double alpha, const double * A, int lda, const double * B, int ldb, double beta, double * C, int ldc)
members.summary: Double-precision general matrix multiply (GEMM).
members.extended:
Identical contract to ``sgemm`` but operates on ``double`` buffers and
dispatches to ``cblas_dgemm``.

**Math**

$$ C_{ij} \leftarrow \alpha \sum_{k=0}^{K-1} A_{ik} B_{kj} + \beta C_{ij} $$

**References**

Accelerate.framework ``cblas_dgemm``.

parameters.name: transA, transB
parameters.description: Whether $A$ or $B$ should be transposed before the multiply.

parameters.name: M, N, K
parameters.description: $C \in \mathbb{R}^{M \times N}$, $A \in \mathbb{R}^{M \times K}$, $B \in \mathbb{R}^{K \times N}$ before optional transposition.

members.name: dgemv
members.signature: void dgemv(bool transA, int M, int N, double alpha, const double * A, int lda, const double * x, int incx, double beta, double * y, int incy)
members.summary: Double-precision general matrix-vector multiply (GEMV).
members.extended:
Identical contract to ``sgemv`` but for ``double`` buffers; dispatches to
``cblas_dgemv``.

**Math**

$$ y_i \leftarrow \alpha \sum_{j=0}^{N-1} A_{ij} x_j + \beta y_i $$

**References**

Accelerate.framework ``cblas_dgemv``.

parameters.name: transA
parameters.description: If true, multiply against $A^T$ instead of $A$.

parameters.name: y
parameters.description: Output vector buffer; updated in place.

members.name: im2col_1d_f32
members.signature: void im2col_1d_f32(const float * x, float * cols, int C, int L, int KL, int OL, int stride_l, int pad_l, int dilation_l)
members.summary: Single-precision 1-D im2col unfold.
members.extended:
Unfolds each length-$KL$ receptive field of a 1-D feature map into a
column of a 2-D matrix so that conv1d can be expressed as a single GEMM.
Out-of-bounds positions (from padding or dilation) are written as zero.

**Math**

For each output index $o \in [0, OL)$, channel $c$ and kernel offset $k$:
$$ \text{cols}[c \cdot KL + k, o] = x[c, o \cdot \text{stride} + k \cdot \text{dilation} - \text{pad}] $$
with zero for out-of-bounds positions.

parameters.name: cols
parameters.description: Output column matrix, shape $(C \cdot KL, OL)$.

parameters.name: stride_l, pad_l, dilation_l
parameters.description: Convolution stride / zero-padding / dilation along the spatial axis.

notes:
Memory layout assumes the standard NCHW-style channel-first convention,
with the batch dimension handled at the caller (loop over $N$).

members.name: im2col_1d_f64
members.signature: void im2col_1d_f64(const double * x, double * cols, int C, int L, int KL, int OL, int stride_l, int pad_l, int dilation_l)
members.summary: Double-precision 1-D im2col unfold.  See ``im2col_1d_f32``.

parameters.name: cols
parameters.description: Output column matrix, shape $(C \cdot KL, OL)$.

parameters.name: C, L, KL, OL
parameters.description: Channel count, input length, kernel length, output length.

members.summary: Single-precision 3-D im2col unfold.
members.extended:
Unfolds a volumetric feature map $x$ of shape $(C, D, H, W)$ into a
column matrix of shape $(C \cdot K_D \cdot K_H \cdot K_W, O_D \cdot O_H
\cdot O_W)$.  Used by conv3d (e.g., for video / medical-imaging models).
Out-of-bounds positions are zero-padded.

parameters.name: x
parameters.description: Input tensor, shape $(C, D, H, W)$ row-major.

notes:
Memory layout is channel-first (NCDHW); batch dimension is iterated by
the caller.

members.summary: Double-precision 3-D im2col unfold.  See ``im2col_3d_f32``.

parameters.name: x

members.name: im2col_f32
members.signature: void im2col_f32(const float * x, float * cols, int C, int H, int W, int KH, int KW, int OH, int OW, int stride_h, int stride_w, int pad_h, int pad_w, int dilation_h, int dilation_w)
members.summary: Single-precision 2-D im2col unfold.
members.extended:
Unfolds the 2-D feature map $x$ of shape $(C, H, W)$ into a matrix where
each column is a $K_H \times K_W$ receptive-field patch flattened across
channels.  The output column matrix has shape $(C \cdot K_H \cdot K_W,
O_H \cdot O_W)$ so that conv2d = ``sgemm(W_reshape, cols)``.

Out-of-bounds positions (those reaching outside the input due to padding,
stride, or dilation) are written as zero.

**Math**

For each output position $(o_h, o_w)$, kernel offset $(k_h, k_w)$ and
channel $c$, the corresponding input position is
$$ i_h = o_h \cdot s_h + k_h \cdot d_h - p_h, \quad i_w = o_w \cdot s_w + k_w \cdot d_w - p_w $$
and ``cols[c \cdot K_H K_W + k_h K_W + k_w, o_h \cdot O_W + o_w] = x[c, i_h, i_w]`` (zero if OOB).

parameters.name: x
parameters.description: Input tensor, shape $(C, H, W)$ row-major.

parameters.name: cols
parameters.description: Output column matrix, shape $(C \cdot K_H \cdot K_W, O_H \cdot O_W)$.

notes:
Memory layout is NCHW (channel-first) — the batch dimension is iterated
at the caller, with one im2col per batch element.

members.summary: Double-precision 2-D im2col unfold.  See ``im2col_f32``.
